original_title: “Mellisai Mannargal” M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy, the ‘Monarchs of Light Music’ in Tamil cinema

en.title: “Mellisai Mannargal” M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy, the ‘Monarchs of Light Music’ in Tamil cinema
en.author: Administrator
body_lines: (June 24th is the 94th birth anniversary of popular music composer Manayangath Subramanian Viswanathan (MSV) who along with  Tiruchirappalli Krishnaswamypillai Ramamurthy (TKR) blazed a musical trail together in Tamil films for a period of 13 years. MS Viswanathan and TK Rammmurthy were known popularly as the “Mellisai Mannargal” (Monarchs of light music).This Article written in July 2015 is being re-posted without any changes to  denote MSV’s 84th Birth Anniversary and revive memories of the music  maestros)

Watching Tamil movies on screen and listening to Tamil film songs over radio was an integral part of life for me as a child growing up in Colombo. The Tamil ‘Varthaga Oliparappu’ or Commercial service of ‘Radio Ceylon’ was a treasure trove of film songs.

Apart from broadcasting the latest Tamil film songs even before the films concerned were screened in India, the commercial service also aired specific programs about films being currently shown in theatres in the island. Popular radio announcer Mailvaganam conducted these programs where film songs were featured regularly. Thus one was able to keep abreast of contemporary Tamil film music courtesy ‘Radio Ceylon’ in those wonderful days of yore.

My sister and I would also sing along with some songs being played out on radio. We were greatly aided in this by what was known as ‘Cinema Paatu Puthagam’ or cinema songbook. These were actually tiny booklets printed on cheap paper with the words of songs in a particular Tamil film. The average Tamil film in those days had about six to nine songs.

Two publishers in Colombo M.K. Ponniahpillai and M.S. Lingam (M. Sockalingam)  printed these songbooks. They were priced from 10 to 25 cents then. I also memorised many film songs from those books – something which stands me in good stead even nowadays while taking a shower at leisure.

All these happy memories of listening to Tamil film songs were kindled in me again as news about the death of a legendary composer of Tamil film music reached me. Music maestro M.S. Viswanathan known widely as “MSV” had passed away at a Chennai hospital in the early hours of the morning on 14 July. He was 87.

MSV has scored music for over 600 films in Tamil. Eighty-six of these film scores were composed jointly with his erstwhile partner T.K. Ramamurthy (TKR). The rest were all solo feats by MSV. Whenever I think of Viswanathan, I am reminded of Ramamurthy. The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo contributed greatly to the Tamil film music by composing popular melodies described as ‘Mellisai’ or light music. Many of their evergreen songs linger in memory still.
Devan Kovil Maniyosai – Live performance with MSV & TKR on stage by Dr. Sirkali SivaChidambaram

A few years back in 2012, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Jayalalithaa Jayaram presided at a ceremony where she conferred the title ‘Thirai Isai Chakkaravarthy’ (Emperor of Screen Music) upon Viswanathan and Ramamurthy. Jayalalithaa was a popular screen heroine in Tamil films before entering politics. Born in 1948 she was part of a generation that grew up with the music of MSV and TKR.

She recalled at the event about how she was influenced by the music of the two. “I grew up listening to the music of these veterans. Their music has become part of my very existence,” the Chief Minister said. Jayalalithaa’s words about the MSV-TKR duo struck a responsive chord in me. As stated earlier relishing Tamil film songs was part of growing up for me and news of MSV’s death does cause a nostalgic void. TKR passed away in 2013.

Manayangath Subramanian Viswanathan (MSV) and Tiruchirappalli Krishnaswamypillai Ramamurthy (TKR) blazed a musical trail together in Tamil films for a period of 13 years. The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo was the Tamil film equivalent to Hindi cinema’s Shankar-Jaikishan. In fact the Shankar-Jaikishan duo was the model which motivated Viswanathan to forge a musical partnership with Ramamurthy.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo came into being in the year 1952. The first film for which the duo composed music was ‘Panam,’ in which noted actors Sivaji Ganesan and Padmini acted together for the first time as hero and heroine. The MSV-TKR duo made musical waves in Tamil cinema until 1965. Their final film together was ‘Aayirathil Oruvan,’ in which M.G. Ramachandran and Jayalalithaa played the lead roles for the first time. Thirty years after the break up, MSV and TKR collaborated again for the film ‘Engirundho Vanthaan’ starring Sathyaraj. MSV and TKR have together composed music for 87 films over a period of 13 years.

Ramamurthy, born in 1922, was the elder of the two. Viswanathan, born in 1928, was six years younger. The two of them became friends while working together in the musical troupe of composer C.R. Subburaman. When Subburaman met with unexpected death at the age of 28 in 1952, Viswanathan and Ramamurthy had to complete their guru’s unfinished work. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy collaborated in composing tunes for a few songs in the films ‘Devadas,’ ‘Kaadhal,’ ‘Sorga Vaasal’ and ‘Chandi Rani’. They did good.

One night while returning after seeing a film at what was then the Midland theatre, Viswanathan suggested that they compose music for films together as equal partners. Ramamurthy demurred saying he was happy to remain a musician. Viswanathan however persuaded Ramamurthy to accept the proposal.

This idea of venturing out as a duo was welcomed by veteran comedian N.S. Krishnan. He hired both as the composers for a film he was producing named ‘Panam’. Viswanathan wanted the partnership to be called the Ramamurthy-Viswanathan duo as TKR was senior in years. N.S. Krishnan however insisted that they should call themselves Viswanathan-Ramamurthy, giving precedence to the younger MSV. Ramamurthy had no objections. Both Viswanathan and Ramamurthy took a vow at the Maariyamman temple in Coimbatore that they would never separate. The MSV-TKR duo was born!

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo made a tremendous contribution to Tamil cinema by popularising film songs. The duo played a significant role in developing and propagating a novel genre of music in films known as ‘Mellisai’ or ‘light music’. This was a refreshing departure from the traditional Carnatic and/or Hindustani classical music forms which hitherto influenced Tamil films.

Music composers began dispensing with some of the orthodox rigidities of classical music in order to increase the appeal of film songs to the average film fan. Such musical compositions were described as ‘light classical music’. Among those who pioneered this type of light classical music in Tamil films were C.R. Subburaman, S.V. Venkatraman, G. Ramanathan, R. Sudharsanam and S.M. Subbiah Naidu.

Viswanathan and Ramamurthy proceeded this light classical music path but added their own particular imprint by emphasising the lighter melodic and rhythmic aspects of music as opposed to the staid classical. Their approach was more flexible being adoptive and adaptive. Even when a classical raga formed the song’s base, the duo modified it on a lighter scale. They introduced other forms of music and wove them seamlessly into the songs. The songs were very often lively melodies with a harmonious blend.

The great Tamil poet Subramania Bharati in one of his verses wished that people would go in all eight directions and fetch the artistic wealth of other countries here (Sendriduveer Ettuthikkum Piranaatuk Kalaichelvangal Konarnthingu Saerppeer).

Bharati was not advocating the plunder of artistic treasures of other countries but merely calling for a transnational artistic exchange. This was exactly what the MSV-TKR duo accomplished. They sought out music forms in all parts of the world and introduced them to Tamil film music.

Viswanathan more than Ramamurthy excelled in such ‘international’ pursuits. It must be noted however that the composer who first introduced non-Indian music into Tamil films was the famous Veena exponent S. Balachandar who scored music for some Tamil films. C.R. Subburaman also followed suit. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy initially emulated Subburaman but soon evolved their own distinctive approach.

The duo went in for a broad range of sounds and styles drawing inspiration from a wide array of musical forms, including dance music. Western classical music, Western country music, gospel songs, ballads, blues, folk music soul music, jazz, waltz, rock and roll, rumba, flamenco, changui, mambo, danzon, guaracha, cha cha cha, lambada, etc. were all utilised. Modifications of Afro-American, Afro-Cuban, Cuban, Latin-American and European musical forms were relied upon greatly for musical inspiration by the duo.

At the same time the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo was firmly rooted to the Tamil musical tradition too. Apart from using modified Carnatic and Hindustani ragas, they also used Tamil rural music. ‘Kummi’ folk beats, ‘Kaavadichchinthu’ ditties, ‘Kilikkanni’ type village songs, ‘Thukkadaa’ classical bits, etc. were also incorporated into their music wherever and whenever appropriate. Eastern and Western music forms were harmoniously blended.

In a sense the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo pioneered fusion music in Tamil cinema. A case in point is the humorous song sequence in the film ‘Pathibakthi’. The song is a combination of rock and roll and Carnatic music. Comedian J.P. Chandrababu sings in his own voice the rock ‘n’ roll opening and related verses. Interspersed are two Carnatic verses in praise of Lord Shiva and Vishnu respectively. The Carnatic component is sung by V.N. Sundaram who voices for T.S. Balaiah in the scene.

In addition to the eclectic taste displayed in seeking inspiration for musical compositions, the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo also began using a wide array of musical instruments in their work. Apart from the regular instruments like harmonium, violin, flute, drum, tabla, piano, veena, etc., the duo also went in for other instruments like the clarinet, cello, oboe, saxophone, trumpet guitar, ukulele mandolin, banjo, piano accordion and bongo drums making much use of them in their scores.

Other composers too have used such instruments in films but the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo utilised them grandly in a manner never done before. Variety in percussion was a particular hallmark of Viswanathan. The tabla and bongos were his favourites.

Apart from African, Latin American and European music, the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo also made use of Arabic, Persian and Afghan music forms. The duo also used North Indian folk music forms. Punjabi Bhangra music and Quawwali Devotional Sufi music were also adapted at times. The duo also used North Indian instruments like the shenoy, sitar, sarangi and sarode for some of their compositions.

The shenoy in particular was used to express pathos in a poignant manner. The song ‘Ullathil Nalla Ullam’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan for the film ‘Karnan’ was composed in the Raga Chakravaham (Ahir Bhairav in Hindustani) by the MSV-TKR duo. The shenoy played by Bismilla Khan is used liberally by the duo to invoke feelings of sympathetic compassion for the tragic Mahabharatha figure Karnan in the death scene.

The same instrument is used by the MSV-TKR duo in the song ‘Aalayamaniyin Osaiyai Naan Kaettaen’ sung by P. Susheela in the film ‘Paalum Palamum’. In this sequence the sounds of the shenoy serve well to bring out the serene mood of a glorious morning as actress Saroja Devi sings on screen.

Ramamurthy was a reputed violinist. Viswanathan played the ‘petti’ as the harmonium was called. Later he mastered piano and keyboard. They would work on a particular tune by playing different versions on their instruments.

Ramamurthy would then write up the notations. They would then sing or play the melodies to their musicians and playback singers. Viswanathan had a flair for composing ear-catching, toe-tapping melodies. Ramamurthy was more of a music arranger working out which instruments were to be used and how. They complemented each other.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy combine used diverse musical instruments in their film compositions. The usage was determined by the nature of the song and sequence. For instance the song ‘Oadam Nathiyiniley’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan in the film ‘Kaathiruntha Kangal’ has only a tabla and two flutes. Another song, ‘Thaalaiyaam Poomudichu’ sung by P. Leela and T.M. Soundararajan in the film ‘Bhagapirivinai,’ also relied on three instruments only.

The same duo however used an orchestra of 300 for the song ‘Engay Nimmathi’ sung by T.M. Soundararajan in ‘Pudhiya Paravai’. Over 100 instruments were used for the Bharatidasan song ‘Sangay Mulangu’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan and P. Susheela in ‘Kalangarai Vilakkam’. Ramamurthy conducted the orchestra on most occasions.

These musical excursions by the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo into what was unchartered territory for Tamil film music at that time brought about a qualitative change in film songs. The melodies though soulful and enchanting yet possessed a captivating common touch.

The film songs too in those days were an integral part of the film and often used by directors to narrate the story effectively. Unlike the present, songs were picturised fully in the films then. The song sequences were part and parcel of the film. In many instances the songs contributed greatly to the commercial success of a film.

The making of a film song was a team effort. The director, lyricist and music composer would discuss among themselves about the film sequence for which a song was required. Except for rare instances, the songs were recorded before the scenes on which they are picturised were shot.

Discussions would determine whether the words were to be written first and tunes composed next or the tunes were to be composed first and words written thereafter. Viswanathan would refer to this process in lighter vein as “matter for metre or metre for matter”.

The MSV-TKR duo succeeded to a great extent in making film songs acceptable and endearing to ordinary filmgoers who began singing those numbers themselves and applying them in their lives. These songs became a part of everyday life itself.

There were songs to denote all aspects or phases of life like birth, death, romance, marriage, heartbreak, separation, reunion, friendship, sorrow, joy celebration, sibling affection, brother-sister bonds, motherly love, husband-wife relationship; there was a Tamil film song for every occasion and every mood in life. In an era where electronics had not invaded every facet of life, there were singsongs galore in many gatherings and Tamil film songs ruled the roost on such occasions.

Even the musical preludes and interludes in a MSV-TKR song were impressive and had an identity of their own. One could recognise a song composed by Viswanathan and Ramamurthy the moment you heard a few lines or musical notes. The 1960 to ’66 period could very well be described as the golden age of Tamil film songs.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy combination along with K.V. Mahadevan dominated the film music scene in that golden age. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy reigned supreme between 1960 and 66. Their refreshing brand of light music or mellisai truly lightened the lives of many.

The popularity of this light music was acknowledged formally on 16 June 1963 at a special event held at the Cultural Academy in Triplicane. The title of ‘Mellisai Mannar’ (Monarch of Light Music) was conferred on both M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy by the doyen of actors, Sivaji Ganesan. A distinguished gathering of film personalities applauded with delight as the two music composers were crowned as monarchs of light music.

Alas! Within two years the relationship fractured. M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy dissolved their musical partnership in July 1965 after the release of the film ‘Aayirathil Oruvan’. Many theories were floated as causes for the break-up. Apparently a clique of film directors, lyricists, playback singers and actors had driven a wedge between both when a clash of egos occurred. Both Viswanathan and Ramamurthy maintained a dignified silence on the matter. There is no doubt however that the split was a great loss to the Tamil film industry. I can recall the immense sadness I felt at the time of the MSV-TKR split.

Both MSV and TKR continued separately on their respective musical journeys. Though both continued to entertain film fans with their light music, their fortunes differed. Ramamurthy found his film career waning after the break up. He began getting fewer and fewer opportunities to score music. TKR did compose music excellently but unfortunately most of the films concerned turned out to be commercial failures. Thus the solo film career of TKR virtually ended within some years. He composed music for only 16 Tamil films after parting ways with MSV. In 1995 the MSV-TKR duo combined again for the film ‘Engiruntho Vanthaan’. The music was disappointing. The old magic was gone.

Contrary to the fate of Ramamurthy, the film career of Viswanathan waxed after he went solo. MSV got a lot of opportunities to score music for films after the split. Possessing remarkable interpersonal skills, Viswanathan was able to procure film assignments easily.

M.S. Viswanathan dominated the Tamil film music scene from 1965 to 1975. He composed music for about 20 films each year during that decade. After the advent of Illayarajah via ‘Annakkili’ in 1976, Viswanathan’s virtual monopoly was challenged. Still Viswanathan held his own against the Illayarajah onslaught until 1985 composing music for at least 10 films each year.

After 1985 the number of films for Viswanathan began to dwindle. It became a trickle after A.R. Rahman also entered the scene in 1992 through ‘Rojah’. The last film for which M.S. Viswanathan composed music was ‘Vaaliban Suttrum Ulagam’ in 2012.

In his boyhood Viswanathan had wanted to be a singer. In his teens he aspired to be an actor. Both dreams did not materialise and Viswanathan became a reputed music composer. Fate however decreed that MSV become a singer and actor in later life.

Viswanathan started singing a few songs in some of his own films first. Later other music composers also began using him. His deep resonating voice was deemed appropriate for philosophical and devotional songs. He has sung over 100 songs in films. Viswanathan has also acted in a few films and teledramas. His film debut was in ‘Kaadhalaa Kaadhalaa,’ starring Kamal Haasan and Prabhu Deva.

M.S. Viswanathan’s death, to use a cliché, marks the end of an era. Though he was inactive as a music composer for nearly two decades, the man’s music and songs continued to be in the public domain thanks to audio and video cassettes and YouTube.
The monarchs of light music M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy brought much pleasure to me and millions of others during their golden days. May their music and memory remain forever!
This article written for the “Spotlight” Column appears in the “Daily FT” of July 18, 2015. It can be accessed here: http://www.ft.lk/d-b-s-jeyaraj

si.title: "මෙල්ලිසායි මන්නාරගල්" එම්.එස්. විශ්වනාදන් සහ ටී.කේ. රාමමූර්ති, දෙමළ සිනමාවේ ‘සැහැල්ලූ සංගීතයේ රජවරු’
si.author: පරිපාලක
body_lines: (June 24th යනු ජනප්‍රිය සංගීත නිර්මාපකයෙකු වන Manayangath Subramanian Viswanathan (MSV) ගේ 94 වන ජන්ම සංවත්සරයයි "Mellisai Mannargal" (සැහැල්ලු සංගීතයේ රජවරු) ලෙස. 2015 ජූලි මාසයේදී ලියන ලද මෙම ලිපිය කිසිදු වෙනසක් නොමැතිව MSV ගේ 84 වන ජන්ම සංවත්සරය දැක්වීමට සහ සංගීත විශාරදයින්ගේ මතකයන් පුනර්ජීවනය කිරීමට නැවත පළ කරනු ලැබේ.

දමිළ චිත්‍රපට තිරයේ නැරඹීමත්, ගුවන් විදුලියෙන් දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත ඇසීමත් කොළඹ හැදී වැඩුණු කුඩා කාලයේ මට ජීවිතයේ අනිවාර්ය අංගයක් විය. දෙමළ ‘වර්තග ඔලිපරප්පු’ හෙවත් ‘රේඩියෝ සිලෝන්’ හි වාණිජ සේවය චිත්‍රපට ගීත නිධානයක් විය.

අදාළ චිත්‍රපට ඉන්දියාවේ ප්‍රදර්ශනය කිරීමට පෙර සිටම නවතම දමිළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත ප්‍රචාරය කිරීමට අමතරව, දැනට දිවයිනේ සිනමාහල්වල ප්‍රදර්ශනය කෙරෙන චිත්‍රපට පිළිබඳ නිශ්චිත වැඩසටහන් ද වාණිජ සේවය විසින් විකාශය කරන ලදී. ජනප්‍රිය ගුවන්විදුලි නිවේදක මයිල්වාගනම් විසින් මෙම වැඩසටහන් මෙහෙය වූ අතර එහිදී චිත්‍රපට ගීත නිරන්තරයෙන් ප්‍රදර්ශනය විය. මේ අනුව, අතීතයේ එම අපූරු දිනවල සමකාලීන දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සංගීතය අනුග්‍රහයෙනි ‘රේඩියෝ සිලෝන්’ සමඟ සමීපව සිටීමට කෙනෙකුට හැකි විය.

ගුවන්විදුලියෙන් වාදනය වන ගීත කිහිපයක් සමඟ මමත් මගේ සහෝදරියත් ගායනා කරනවා. ‘සිනමා පාතු පුත්‍රගම්’ හෙවත් සිනමා ගී පොත අපට මේ සඳහා බෙහෙවින් උපකාර විය. මේවා ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම යම් දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටයක ගීතවල වචන සහිත ලාභ කඩදාසි මත මුද්‍රණය කරන ලද කුඩා පොත් පිංච විය. ඒ දවස්වල සාමාන්‍ය දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටයක ගීත හයක් නවයක් විතර තිබුණා.

කොළඹ ප්‍රකාශකයින් දෙදෙනෙකු වන එම්.කේ. පොන්නයියාපිල්ලේ සහ එම්. ලිංගම් (එම්. සොකලිංගම්) මෙම ගීත පොත් මුද්‍රණය කළේය. එකල ඒවායේ මිල ශත 10 සිට 25 දක්වා විය. ඒ පොත්වලින් චිත්‍රපට ගීත රාශියක් මට කටපාඩම් කළා - අද කාලයේ පවා විවේකීව ස්නානය කරන විට මට හොඳ ස්ථාවරයක් ගෙන දෙන දෙයක්.

දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත ශ්‍රවණය කිරීමේ මේ සතුටුදායක මතකයන් යළිත් මා තුළ දැල්වුණේ දමිළ සිනමාවේ ජනප්‍රිය සංගීත නිර්මාණකරුවකුගේ අභාවය පිළිබඳ පුවත මා වෙත වාර්තා වීමත් සමඟය. සංගීත විශාරද එම්.එස්. "MSV" ලෙසින් ප්‍රචලිත වූ විශ්වනාදන් ජුලි 14 දින අලුයම චෙන්නායි රෝහලකදී අභාවප්‍රාප්ත විය. ඔහු 87 විය.

MSV දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට 600 කට අධික සංඛ්‍යාවකට සංගීතය ලබා දී ඇත. මෙම චිත්‍රපට ලකුණු අසූහයම ඔහුගේ පැරණි සහකරු T.K සමඟ එක්ව රචනා කරන ලදී. රාමමූර්ති (TKR). ඉතිරි සියල්ල MSV ගේ ඒකල දස්කම් විය. විශ්වනාදන් ගැන මතක් වෙන හැම වෙලාවකම මට මතක් වෙන්නේ රාමමූර්ති. විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සංගීතයට විශාල දායකත්වයක් ලබා දුන්නේ ‘මෙල්ලිසයි’ හෙවත් සැහැල්ලු සංගීතය ලෙසින් විස්තර කෙරෙන ජනප්‍රිය තනු නිර්මාණය කිරීමෙනි. ඔවුන්ගේ සදාහරිත ගීත බොහොමයක් තවමත් මතකයේ රැඳේ.
දේවන් කෝවිල් මනියෝසෙයි - වේදිකාවේ MSV සහ TKR සමඟ සජීවී ප්‍රසංගය ආචාර්ය සර්කාලි සිවචිදම්බරම් විසිනි

වසර කිහිපයකට පෙර 2012 දී තමිල්නාඩු මහ ඇමතිනී ජයලලිතා ජයරාම් විසින් විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති වෙත ‘තිරයි ඉසයි චක්කරවර්ති’ (තිර සංගීතයේ අධිරාජයා) යන පදවි නාමය ප්‍රදානය කරන ලද උත්සවයකදී ප්‍රධානත්වයෙන් පැවැත්විණි. ජයලලිතා දේශපාලනයට පැමිණීමට පෙර දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල ජනප්‍රිය තිරයේ වීරවරියකි. 1948 දී උපත ලද ඇය MSV සහ TKR සංගීතය සමඟ හැදී වැඩුණු පරම්පරාවක කොටසක් විය.

ඔවුන් දෙදෙනාගේ සංගීතය තමාට බලපෑ ආකාරය ගැන ඇය එම උත්සවයේදී සිහිපත් කළාය. “මම හැදී වැඩුණේ මේ ප්‍රවීණයන්ගේ සංගීතයට සවන් දෙමින්. ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීතය මගේ පැවැත්මේම කොටසක් බවට පත්වෙලා.” මහ ඇමැතිවරයා කීවේය. MSV-TKR යුගලය ගැන ජයලලිතාගේ වදන් මා තුළ ප්‍රතිචාරාත්මක ගීතයක් ඇති කළේය. කලින් ප්‍රකාශ කළ පරිදි දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත රස විඳීම මට හැදී වැඩීමේ කොටසක් වූ අතර MSV ගේ මරණය පිළිබඳ පුවත නොස්ටැල්ජික් හිස්බවක් ඇති කරයි. TKR 2013 දී මිය ගියේය.

මනයංගත් සුබ්‍රමනියන් විශ්වනාදන් (MSV) සහ තිරුචිරප්පල්ලි ක්‍රිෂ්ණස්වාමිපිල්ලේ රාමමූර්ති (TKR) වසර 13 ක කාලයක් දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල සංගීත මාවතක් දැල්වීය. විශ්වනාදන්-රාමමූර්ති ද්විත්වය හින්දි සිනමාවේ ශංකර්-ජයිකිෂාන් චිත්‍රපටයට සමාන දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටයක් විය. ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම ශංකර්-ජයිකිෂන් යුගලය රාමමූර්ති සමඟ සංගීත සහයෝගීතාවයක් ඇති කර ගැනීමට විශ්වනාදන් පෙලඹවූ ආකෘතියයි.

විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය බිහි වූයේ 1952 වර්ෂයේදීය. ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා සංගීතවත් කළ පළමු චිත්‍රපටය වූයේ ප්‍රකට නළුවන් වන සිවාජි ගනේෂන් සහ පද්මිණී ප්‍රථම වරට වීරයා සහ වීරවරිය ලෙස එකට රඟපෑ ‘පානම්’ චිත්‍රපටයයි. MSV-TKR යුගලය 1965 දක්වා දෙමළ සිනමාවේ සංගීත රැල්ලක් ඇති කළේය. ඔවුන්ගේ අවසන් චිත්‍රපටය වූයේ ‘අයිරතිල් ඔරුවන්’ වන අතර එහි එම්.ජී. රාමචන්ද්‍රන් සහ ජයලලිතා ප්‍රථම වරට ප්‍රධාන චරිත නිරූපණය කළහ. වෙන්වීමෙන් වසර තිහකට පසු, MSV සහ TKR නැවතත් සත්‍යරාජ් ප්‍රධාන චරිතය රඟපෑ ‘එංගිරුන්දෝ වන්තාන්’ චිත්‍රපටය සඳහා සහයෝගයෙන් කටයුතු කළහ. MSV සහ TKR එක්ව වසර 13ක කාලයක් තුළ චිත්‍රපට 87ක් සඳහා සංගීත නිර්මාණය කර ඇත.

1922 දී උපන් රාමමූර්ති ඔවුන් දෙදෙනාගෙන් වැඩිමලා විය. 1928 උපන් විශ්වනාදන් අවුරුදු හයකින් බාල විය. ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා මිත්‍රත්වයට පත්වූයේ සංගීත නිර්මාපකයෙකු වන C.R. Subburaman ගේ සංගීත කණ්ඩායමේ එකට වැඩ කරන අතරතුරයි. 1952 දී සුබුරාමන් වයස අවුරුදු 28 දී අනපේක්ෂිත මරණයකට මුහුණ දුන් විට, විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්තිට ඔවුන්ගේ ගුරුවරයාගේ නිම නොකළ වැඩ නිම කිරීමට සිදු විය. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති එක්ව ‘දේවදාස්,’ ‘කාදල්,’ ‘සෝර්ගා වාසල්’ සහ ‘චණ්ඩි රාණි’ යන චිත්‍රපටවල ගීත කිහිපයක් සඳහා තනු නිර්මාණය කළහ. ඔවුන් හොඳ කළා.

එක් රාත්‍රියක එවකට මිඩ්ලන්ඩ් රඟහලේ චිත්‍රපටයක් නරඹා ආපසු එන අතරතුර විශ්වනාදන් යෝජනා කළේ සමාන හවුල්කරුවන් ලෙස එක්ව චිත්‍රපට සඳහා සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කරන ලෙසයි. රාමමූර්ති ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළේ තමා සංගීතඥයෙකු ලෙස සිටීම ගැන සතුටු වන බව පවසමිනි. කෙසේ වෙතත් විශ්වනාදන් එම යෝජනාව පිළිගන්නා ලෙස රාමමූර්තිව පොළඹවා ගත්තේය.

යුගලයක් ලෙස ඉදිරියට යාමේ මෙම අදහස ප්‍රවීණ විකට නළු එන්.එස්. ක්‍රිෂ්ණන්. ඔහු නිෂ්පාදනය කරන ‘පානම්’ නම් චිත්‍රපටයේ තනු නිර්මාණකරුවන් ලෙස දෙදෙනාම බඳවා ගත්තේය. TKR වසර ගණනාවකින් ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ වූ බැවින් මෙම හවුල්කාරිත්වය රාමමූර්ති-විශ්වනාදන් යුගල ලෙස හැඳින්වීමට විශ්වනාදන්ට අවශ්‍ය විය. එන්.එස්. කෙසේ වෙතත් ක්‍රිෂ්ණන් අවධාරනය කළේ ඔවුන් තමන්ව විශ්වනාදන්-රාමමූර්ති ලෙස හැඳින්විය යුතු බව ය. රාමමූර්තිට විරුද්ධත්වයක් තිබුණේ නැත. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති යන දෙදෙනාම කොයිම්බතූර්හි මාරියම්මන් කෝවිලේදී කිසිදා වෙන් නොවන බවට දිවුරුම් දුන්හ. MSV-TKR යුගලය උපත ලැබීය!

විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය චිත්‍රපට ගීත ජනප්‍රිය කරමින් දෙමළ සිනමාවට ඉමහත් මෙහෙවරක් කළා. ‘මෙල්ලිසයි’ හෝ ‘සැහැල්ලූ සංගීතය’ ලෙසින් හැඳින්වෙන චිත්‍රපටවල නවකතා සංගීත ප්‍රභේදයක් වර්ධනය කිරීම සහ ප්‍රචාරණය කිරීම සඳහා ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා සැලකිය යුතු කාර්යභාරයක් ඉටු කළහ. මෙය මෙතෙක් දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවලට බලපෑ සාම්ප්‍රදායික කර්නාටක සහ/හෝ හින්දුස්ථානි ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීත ආකෘතිවලින් ප්‍රබෝධමත් වීමකි.

සාමාන්‍ය චිත්‍රපට රසිකයින් වෙත චිත්‍රපට ගීත ආකර්ෂණය වැඩි කිරීම සඳහා සංගීත නිර්මාපකයින් සම්භාව්‍ය සංගීතයේ සමහර සම්ප්‍රදායික දෘඩතාවයන් බෙදා හැරීමට පටන් ගත්හ. එවැනි සංගීත රචනා විස්තර කළේ 'සැහැල්ලු ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීතය' ලෙසයි. දෙමළ චිත්‍රපටවල මේ ආකාරයේ සැහැල්ලු ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීතය පුරෝගාමී වූවන් අතර සී.ආර්.සුබ්බුරාමන්, එස්.වී. වෙන්කට්රාමන්, ජී. රාමනාදන්, ආර්. සුදර්ශනම් සහ එස්.එම්. Subbiah Naidu.

විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති මෙම සැහැල්ලු ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීත මාවතේ ගමන් කළ නමුත් ස්ථීර ශාස්ත්‍රීය සංගීතයට වඩා සැහැල්ලු සංගීතමය සහ රිද්මයානුකූල සංගීතය අවධාරණය කරමින් ඔවුන්ටම ආවේණික වූ මුද්‍රාවක් එක් කළහ. ඔවුන්ගේ ප්‍රවේශය වඩාත් නම්‍යශීලී වූයේ දරුකමට හදාගැනීම සහ අනුවර්තනය වීමයි. සම්භාව්‍ය රාගයක් ගීතයේ පදනම සැකසූ විට පවා, දෙදෙනා එය සැහැල්ලු පරිමාණයකින් වෙනස් කළහ. ඔවුන් වෙනත් සංගීත ආකෘතීන් හඳුන්වා දුන් අතර ඒවා ගීතවලට බාධාවකින් තොරව වියන ලදී. එම ගීත බොහෝ විට සුසංයෝගී මිශ්‍රණයකින් යුත් සජීවී තනු නිර්මාණය විය.

ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨ දමිළ කවියෙකු වූ සුබ්‍රමනිය භාරතී සිය එක් පද්‍යයක ප්‍රාර්ථනා කළේ මිනිසුන් අට දිසාවට ගොස් වෙනත් රටවල කලා ධනය මෙහි ගෙන එනු ඇතැයි (සෙන්ද්‍රිදුවීර් එත්තුතික්කුම් පිරනාටුක් කලයිචෙල්වන්ගල් කොනර්න්තිංගු සාර්පීර්) ය.

භාරතී වෙනත් රටවල කලාත්මක වස්තු කොල්ලකෑම වෙනුවෙන් පෙනී නොසිටි අතර හුදෙක් අන්තර්ජාතික කලාත්මක හුවමාරුවක් ඉල්ලා සිටියේය. MSV-TKR යුගලය ඉටු කළේ මෙයයි. ඔවුන් ලෝකයේ සෑම තැනකම සංගීත ආකෘති සොයාගෙන ඒවා දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සංගීතයට හඳුන්වා දුන්හ.

රාමමූර්තිට වඩා විශ්වනාදන් එවැනි ‘ජාත්‍යන්තර’ ලුහුබැඳීම්වල දක්‍ෂ විය. කෙසේ වෙතත්, දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සඳහා ඉන්දියානු නොවන සංගීතය ප්‍රථම වරට හඳුන්වා දුන් නිර්මාපකයා වූයේ සමහර දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සඳහා සංගීතය කළ සුප්‍රසිද්ධ වීණා වාදක එස්. බාලචන්දර් බව සඳහන් කළ යුතුය. C.R Subburaman ද එය අනුගමනය කළේය. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති මුලින් සුබුරාමන්ව අනුකරණය කළ නමුත් ඉක්මනින්ම ඔවුන්ගේම සුවිශේෂී ප්‍රවේශය විකාශනය කළහ.

ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා නර්තන සංගීතය ඇතුළු පුළුල් පරාසයක සංගීත ස්වරූපවලින් ආශ්වාදයක් ලබා ගනිමින් පුළුල් පරාසයක ශබ්ද සහ ශෛලීන් සඳහා යොමු වූහ. බටහිර සම්භාව්‍ය සංගීතය, බටහිර රට සංගීතය, ශුභාරංචි ගීත, බැලඩ්ස්, බ්ලූස්, ජන සංගීතය ආත්ම සංගීතය, ජෑස්, වෝල්ට්ස්, රොක් ඇන්ඩ් රෝල්, රම්බා, ෆ්ලමෙන්කෝ, චංගුයි, මාම්බෝ, ඩැන්සන්, ගුරාචා, චා චා චා, ලැම්බඩා යනාදිය සියල්ලම විය. භාවිතා කර ඇත. Afro-American, Afro-Cuban, Cuban, Latin-American සහ European musical forms හි වෙනස් කිරීම් මෙම දෙදෙනාගේ සංගීත ආශ්වාදය සඳහා බෙහෙවින් රඳා පැවතුනි.

ඒ අතරම විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය දෙමළ සංගීත සම්ප්‍රදායට ද තදින් මුල් බැස ගත්හ. නවීකරණය කරන ලද කර්නාටක සහ හින්දුස්ථානි රාගවලට අමතරව ඔවුන් දෙමළ ග්‍රාමීය සංගීතය ද භාවිතා කළහ. ‘කුම්මි’ ජනකතා, ‘කාවඩිච්චිංතු’ දිට්ඨි, ‘කිලික්කන්නි’ වර්ගයේ ගැමි ගීත, ‘තුක්කඩ’ සම්භාව්‍ය ගීත, යනාදිය ද ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීතයට සුදුසු තැන්වල සහ සෑම විටම ඇතුළත් විය. පෙරදිග හා බටහිර සංගීත ආකෘතීන් සුසංයෝගයෙන් මුසු විය.

එක් අතකින් විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය දෙමළ සිනමාවේ විලයන සංගීතයේ පුරෝගාමී විය. ‘පතිභක්ති’ චිත්‍රපටයේ හාස්‍යජනක ගීත මාලාව ඊට නිදසුනකි. ගීතය රොක් ඇන්ඩ් රෝල් සහ කර්නාටක සංගීතයේ සංකලනයකි. විකට නළු J.P. චන්ද්‍රබාබු ඔහුගේම හඬින් රොක් ‘එන්’ රෝල් විවෘත කිරීම සහ ඒ ආශ්‍රිත පද ගායනා කරයි. පිළිවෙළින් ශිව දෙවියන්ට සහ විෂ්ණු දෙවියන්ට ප්‍රශංසා කරන කර්නාටක පද දෙකක් අතරට ගොස් ඇත. කර්නාටක සංරචකය ගායනා කරනු ලබන්නේ V.N. ටී.එස් වෙනුවෙන් හඬ නඟන සුන්දරම්. බාලයියා දර්ශනයේ.

සංගීත රචනා සඳහා ආභාසය සෙවීමේදී ප්‍රදර්ශනය කරන ලද විචිත්‍රවත් රසයට අමතරව, විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය ඔවුන්ගේ වැඩ සඳහා පුළුල් සංගීත භාණ්ඩ මාලාවක් ද භාවිතා කිරීමට පටන් ගත්හ. හාර්මෝනියම්, වයලීනය, නළාව, බෙරය, තබ්ලා, පියානෝ, වීණා ආදී සාමාන්‍ය භාණ්ඩවලට අමතරව ක්ලැරිනට්, සෙලෝ, ඕබෝ, සැක්සෆෝන්, ට්‍රම්පට් ගිටාරය, උකුලේලේ මැන්ඩලින්, බැන්ජෝ, පියානෝ වැනි වාද්‍ය භාණ්ඩ සඳහා ද මේ දෙපළ යොමු වූහ. Accordion සහ bongo drums ඔවුන්ගේ ලකුණු වලදී ඒවා බොහෝ සෙයින් භාවිතා කරයි.

වෙනත් නිර්මාපකයින් ද චිත්‍රපට සඳහා එවැනි උපකරණ භාවිතා කර ඇති නමුත් විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති යුගලය මින් පෙර කිසි දිනෙක නොකළ ආකාරයෙන් ඒවා විශිෂ්ට ලෙස භාවිතා කළහ. බෙර වාදනයේ විවිධත්වය විශ්වනාදන්ගේ විශේෂ ලක්ෂණයක් විය. තබ්ලා සහ බොංගෝ ඔහුගේ ප්‍රියතම ඒවා විය.

අප්‍රිකානු, ලතින් ඇමරිකානු සහ යුරෝපීය සංගීතයට අමතරව, විශ්වනාදන්-රාමමූර්ති යුගලය අරාබි, පර්සියානු සහ ඇෆ්ගන් සංගීත ආකෘති ද භාවිතා කළහ. ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා උතුරු ඉන්දියානු ජන සංගීත ආකෘති ද භාවිතා කළහ. පන්ජාබි භන්ග්‍රා සංගීතය සහ ක්වාවාලි භක්තිමත් සුෆි සංගීතය ද ඇතැම් විට අනුවර්තනය විය. ඔවුන් දෙදෙනා ඔවුන්ගේ සමහර රචනා සඳහා ෂෙනෝයි, සිතාර්, සාරංගි සහ සරොදේ වැනි උතුරු ඉන්දියානු සංගීත භාණ්ඩ ද භාවිතා කළහ.

විශේෂයෙන් ෂෙනෝයි භාවිතා කළේ ව්‍යාකූල ලෙස ප්‍රකාශ කිරීමට ය. ‘කර්නන්’ චිත්‍රපටය සඳහා සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන් විසින් ගායනා කරන ලද ‘උල්ලතිල් නල්ල උල්ලම්’ ගීතය MSV-TKR යුගලය විසින් රාග චක්‍රවාහම් (හින්දුස්ථානි භාෂාවෙන් Ahir Bhairav) විසින් රචනා කරන ලදී. බිස්මිලා ඛාන් විසින් වාදනය කරන ලද ෂෙනෝයි මරණ දර්ශනයේ ඛේදජනක මහා භාරත චරිතයක් වන කර්නන් කෙරෙහි සානුකම්පිත දයානුකම්පාවේ හැඟීම් ඇති කිරීමට දෙදෙනා විසින් බහුල ලෙස භාවිතා කරයි.

‘පාලුම් පාලමම්’ චිත්‍රපටයේ පී.සුශීලා ගායනා කරන ‘ආලයමනියෙන් ඔසයියයි නාන් කැත්තෙන්’ ගීතයේ MSV-TKR යුගලය භාවිතා කරන්නේද එම සංගීත භාණ්ඩයයි. මෙම අනුපිළිවෙලෙහි, නිළි සරෝජා දේවි තිරයේ ගායනා කරන විට, තේජාන්විත උදෑසනක සන්සුන් මනෝභාවය ගෙන ඒමට ෂෙනෝයිගේ ශබ්දය හොඳින් සේවය කරයි.

රාමමූර්ති කීර්තිමත් වයලීන වාදකයෙක් විය. විශ්වනාදන් හාර්මෝනියම් හැඳින්වූ පරිදි ‘පෙට්ටි’ වාදනය කළේය. පසුව ඔහු පියානෝව සහ යතුරුපුවරුව ප්‍රගුණ කළේය. ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීත භාණ්ඩවල විවිධ අනුවාද වාදනය කිරීමෙන් ඔවුන් විශේෂිත තාලයක් මත වැඩ කරනු ඇත.

රාමමූර්ති පසුව සටහන් ලියයි. ඔවුන් පසුව ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීතඥයින්ට සහ පසුබිම් ගායකයින්ට තනු ගායනා හෝ වාදනය කරනු ඇත. කන්වලට හසුවන, පයේ ඇඟිලිවලට තට්ටු කරන තනු නිර්මාණය කිරීමේ දක්ෂතාවක් විශ්වනාදන් සතු විය. රාමමූර්ති වැඩිපුරම සංගීත සංයෝජකයෙකු වූයේ කුමන සංගීත භාණ්ඩ භාවිතා කළ යුතුද සහ කෙසේද යන්න සොයා බැලීමයි. ඔවුන් එකිනෙකාට අනුපූරක විය.

විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති සංයෝජනය ඔවුන්ගේ චිත්‍රපට රචනා සඳහා විවිධ සංගීත භාණ්ඩ භාවිතා කළහ. ගීතයේ ස්වභාවය සහ අනුපිළිවෙල අනුව භාවිතය තීරණය විය. උදාහරණයක් ලෙස ‘කාතිරුන්ත කන්ගල්’ චිත්‍රපටයේ සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන් ගැයූ ‘ඔදම් නැතිනිලේ’ ගීතයේ ඇත්තේ තබ්ලාවක් සහ නළා දෙකක් පමණි. පී.ලීලා සහ ටී.එම්. විසින් ගායනා කරන ලද තවත් ගීතයක් වන ‘තාලෙයියම් පූමුඩිචු’ ‘භාගපිරිවිනයි’ චිත්‍රපටයේ සෞන්දරරාජන් ද වාද්‍ය භාණ්ඩ තුනක් මත විශ්වාසය තැබුවේය.

කෙසේ වෙතත් එම දෙපළම ටී.එම් විසින් ගායනා කරන ලද 'එංගේ නිම්මති' ගීතය සඳහා 300 ක වාද්‍ය වෘන්දයක් භාවිතා කළහ. සෞන්දරරාජන් ‘පුදිය පරවයි’ හි. ‘කලංගරයි විලක්කම්’ හි සීර්ගලී ගෝවින්දරාජන් සහ පී.සුශීලා විසින් ගායනා කරන ලද ‘සංගයි මුලංගු’ භාරතීදාසන් ගීතය සඳහා සංගීත භාණ්ඩ 100 කට අධික සංඛ්‍යාවක් භාවිතා කරන ලදී. රාමමූර්ති බොහෝ අවස්ථාවල වාද්‍ය වෘන්දය මෙහෙයවීය.

විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති දෙපළ විසින් එකල දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සංගීතය සඳහා වරප්‍රසාද නොලද ප්‍රදේශයක් වූ මෙම සංගීත විනෝද චාරිකා චිත්‍රපට ගීතවල ගුණාත්මක වෙනසක් ඇති කළේය. තනුවලට ප්‍රාණවත් හා සිත් ඇදගන්නාසුළු වුවද සිත් ඇදගන්නා පොදු ස්පර්ශයක් තිබුණි.

ඒ දවස්වල චිත්‍රපට ගීතද චිත්‍රපටයේ අනිවාර්ය අංගයක් වූ අතර බොහෝ විට අධ්‍යක්ෂවරුන් විසින් කතාව ඵලදායි ලෙස පැවසීමට යොදා ගන්නා ලදී. වර්තමානයේ මෙන් නොව එදා චිත්‍රපටවල ගීත සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම රූපගත විය. ගීත අනුපිළිවෙල චිත්‍රපටයේ කොටසක් විය. බොහෝ අවස්ථාවල චිත්‍රපටයක වාණිජමය සාර්ථකත්වයට එම ගීත බෙහෙවින් දායක විය.

චිත්‍රපට ගීතයක් නිර්මාණය කිරීම කණ්ඩායම් උත්සාහයක්. ගීතයක් අවශ්‍ය චිත්‍රපට අනුපිළිවෙල ගැන අධ්‍යක්ෂක, ගේය පද රචක සහ සංගීත නිර්මාපකයෝ එකිනෙකා අතර සාකච්ඡා කළහ. දුර්ලභ අවස්ථා හැර, ගීත රූපගත කර ඇති දර්ශන රූගත කිරීමට පෙර පටිගත කරන ලදී.

වචන මුලින් ලිවිය යුතුද ඊළඟට තනුව සෑදිය යුතුද නැතහොත් තනුව පළමුව රචනා කර පසුව වචන ලිවිය යුතුද යන්න සාකච්ඡා මගින් තීරණය වේ. විශ්වනාදන් මෙම ක්‍රියාවලිය සැහැල්ලු ශිරා ලෙස හඳුන්වනු ඇත්තේ “මීටරයට පදාර්ථය හෝ පදාර්ථයට මීටරය” ලෙසිනි.

MSV-TKR යුගලය චිත්‍රපට ගීත පිළිගත හැකි සහ ප්‍රියජනක කිරීමට බොහෝ දුරට සමත් වූ අතර එම අංක තමන් විසින්ම ගායනා කිරීමට පටන් ගත් සාමාන්‍ය චිත්‍රපට ප්‍රේක්ෂකයින්ට ඒවා තම ජීවිතයට අදාළ කර ගැනීමට පටන් ගත්හ. මෙම ගීත එදිනෙදා ජීවිතයේ කොටසක් බවට පත් විය.

උපත, මරණය, ප්‍රේමය, විවාහය, හද කම්පාව, වෙන්වීම, නැවත එක්වීම, මිත්‍රත්වය, දුක, ප්‍රීතිය සැමරීම, සහෝදර සෙනෙහස, සහෝදර සහෝදරියන්ගේ බැඳීම්, මව් සෙනෙහස, සැමියා-බිරිඳ සම්බන්ධය වැනි ජීවිතයේ සෑම අංගයක් හෝ අවධීන් හඟවන්න ගීත තිබුණා. ජීවිතයේ සෑම අවස්ථාවකටම සහ සෑම මනෝභාවයකටම දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීතයක් තිබුණි. ජීවිතයේ සෑම අංශයක්ම ඉලෙක්ට්‍රොනික උපකරණ ආක්‍රමණය කර නොතිබූ යුගයක, බොහෝ රැස්වීම් වල ගායක ගායනය වූ අතර දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීත එවැනි අවස්ථාවන්හිදී පාලනය විය.

MSV-TKR ගීතයක සංගීතමය පෙරවදන සහ අතුරු කථා පවා සිත් ඇදගන්නාසුළු වූ අතර ඒවාට තමන්ගේම අනන්‍යතාවයක් තිබුණි. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති විසින් රචනා කරන ලද ගීතයක් ඔබට පද පේළි කිහිපයක් හෝ සංගීත සටහන් ඇසුණු සැණින් කෙනෙකුට හඳුනාගත හැකිය. 1960 සිට 66 දක්වා කාලය දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට ගීතවල ස්වර්ණමය යුගය ලෙස හැඳින්විය හැකිය.

විශ්වනාදන් - රාමමූර්ති සංයෝජනය කේ.වී. ඒ ස්වර්ණමය යුගයේ සිනමා සංගීත ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ ආධිපත්‍යය දැරුවේ මහාදේවන්. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති 1960 සහ 66 අතර උත්තරීතර රජකම් කළහ. ඔවුන්ගේ ප්‍රබෝධමත් සන්නාමය වූ සැහැල්ලු සංගීතය නොහොත් මෙලිසායි සැබවින්ම බොහෝ දෙනෙකුගේ ජීවිත ආලෝකවත් කළේය.

මෙම සැහැල්ලු සංගීතයේ ජනප්‍රියත්වය 1963 ජුනි 16 වන දින ට්‍රිප්ලිකේන් හි සංස්කෘතික ඇකඩමියේ පැවති විශේෂ උත්සවයකදී විධිමත් ලෙස පිළිගනු ලැබීය. ‘මෙල්ලිසයි මන්නාරම’ (සැහැල්ලු සංගීතයේ අධිරාජයා) යන නාමය එම්.එස්. විශ්වනාදන් සහ ටී.කේ. රාමමූර්ති විසින් නළු නිළියන් වන සිවාජි ගනේෂන් විසිනි. සංගීත නිර්මාපකයින් දෙදෙනා සැහැල්ලු සංගීතයේ රජවරුන් ලෙස කිරුළු පැලඳීමත් සමඟ කීර්තිමත් සිනමා පෞරුෂයන් ප්‍රීතියෙන් අත්පොළසන් දුන්හ.

අහෝ! වසර දෙකක් ඇතුළත සම්බන්ධතාවය බිඳී ගියේය. මෙනෙවිය. විශ්වනාදන් සහ ටී.කේ. රාමමූර්ති 1965 ජූලි මාසයේදී ‘ආයිරතිල් ඔරුවන්’ චිත්‍රපටිය තිරගත වීමෙන් පසු ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීත සහයෝගීතාව විසුරුවා හැරියේය. වෙන්වීමට හේතු ලෙස බොහෝ න්‍යායන් ඉදිරිපත් විය. පෙනෙන විදිහට චිත්‍රපට අධ්‍යක්ෂවරුන්, ගී පද රචකයන්, පසුබිම් ගායකයින් සහ නළු නිළියන් සමූහයක් ආත්මාර්ථකාමී ගැටුමක් ඇති වූ විට දෙදෙනා අතර විරසකයක් ඇති කර තිබේ. විශ්වනාදන් සහ රාමමූර්ති යන දෙදෙනාම මේ කාරණය සම්බන්ධයෙන් ගෞරවාන්විත නිශ්ශබ්දතාවයක් රකින ලදී. කෙසේ වෙතත් එම භේදය දෙමළ සිනමා කර්මාන්තයට විශාල පාඩුවක් වූ බවට සැකයක් නැත. MSV-TKR භේදය සිදු වූ අවස්ථාවේ මට දැනුණු ඉමහත් දුක මට මතකයි.

MSV සහ TKR යන දෙදෙනාම ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීත චාරිකාවල වෙන වෙනම ඉදිරියට ගියහ. දෙදෙනාම සිය සැහැල්ලූ සංගීතයෙන් චිත්‍රපට රසිකයින් පිනවීමට දිගින් දිගටම කටයුතු කළද ඔවුන්ගේ වාසනාව වෙනස් විය. රාමමූර්ති විරහවෙන් පසු ඔහුගේ සිනමා දිවිය පිරිහෙමින් තිබෙනු දුටුවේය. ඔහුට සංගීතය සඳහා අවස්ථා අඩු හා අඩු වීමට පටන් ගත්තේය. TKR විශිෂ්ට ලෙස සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කළ නමුත් අවාසනාවකට අදාළ චිත්‍රපට බොහොමයක් වාණිජමය වශයෙන් අසාර්ථක විය. මේ අනුව TKR හි ඒකල චිත්‍රපට ජීවිතය වසර කිහිපයක් ඇතුළත පාහේ අවසන් විය. එම්.එස්.වී ගෙන් වෙන්වීමෙන් පසු ඔහු සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කළේ දමිළ චිත්‍රපට 16කට පමණි. 1995 දී MSV-TKR යුගලය ‘Engiruntho Vanthaan’ චිත්‍රපටිය සඳහා නැවතත් එකතු විය. සංගීතය බලාපොරොත්තු සුන් විය. පැරණි මායාව නැති විය.

රාමමූර්තිගේ ඉරණමට පටහැනිව, ඔහු තනිව ගිය පසු විශ්වනාදන්ගේ සිනමා ජීවිතය ඉටිපන්දම් විය. MSV වෙන්වීමෙන් පසු චිත්‍රපට සඳහා සංගීතය කිරීමට බොහෝ අවස්ථාවන් ලැබුණි. කැපී පෙනෙන අන්තර් පුද්ගල කුසලතා ඇති විශ්වනාදන් පහසුවෙන් චිත්‍රපට පැවරුම් ලබා ගැනීමට සමත් විය.

මෙනෙවිය. විශ්වනාදන් 1965 සිට 1975 දක්වා දෙමළ චිත්‍රපට සංගීත ක්ෂේත්‍රයේ ආධිපත්‍යය දැරීය. එම දශකය තුළ ඔහු සෑම වසරකම චිත්‍රපට 20 කට පමණ සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කළේය. 1976 දී ‘අන්නක්කිලි’ හරහා ඉලයරාජාගේ ආගමනයෙන් පසු, විශ්වනාදන්ගේ අතථ්‍ය ඒකාධිකාරය අභියෝගයට ලක් විය. විශ්වනාදන් 1985 වසර දක්වා අඩුම තරමින් සෑම වසරකම චිත්‍රපට 10 කටවත් සංගීතය නිර්මාණය කරන තෙක් ඉලයරාජා ප්‍රහාරයට එරෙහිව ඔහුගේම ධෛර්‍ය සම්පන්නව සිටියේය.

1985 න් පසු විශ්වනාදන්ට චිත්‍රපට ප්‍රමාණය අඩු වන්නට විය. ඒ.ආර්.ට පස්සේ ඒක ට්‍රික්ක් වුණා. රහ්මාන් ද 1992 වසරේ ‘රෝජා’ හරහා රංගනයට පිවිසියේය. එම්.එස්. විශ්වනාදන් සංගීතවත් කළේ 2012 වසරේ ‘වාලිබන් සූත්‍රම් උලගම්’ චිත්‍රපටයයි.

විශ්වනාදන්ට ඔහුගේ කුඩා කාලයේදීම අවශ්‍ය වී තිබුණේ ගායකයෙකු වීමටය. නව යොවුන් වියේදී ඔහු නළුවෙකු වීමට අපේක්ෂා කළේය. සිහින දෙකම සැබෑ නොවූ අතර විශ්වනාදන් කීර්තිමත් සංගීත නිර්මාපකයෙකු බවට පත්විය. කෙසේ වෙතත් දෛවය විසින් MSV පසුකාලීන ජීවිතයේ ගායකයෙකු හා නළුවෙකු බවට පත් විය.

විශ්වනාදන් මුලින්ම ඔහුගේම චිත්‍රපට කිහිපයක ගීත කිහිපයක් ගායනා කිරීමට පටන් ගත්තේය. පසුව වෙනත් සංගීත නිර්මාණකරුවන් ද ඔහුව භාවිතා කිරීමට පටන් ගත්හ. ඔහුගේ ගැඹුරු අනුනාද හඬ දාර්ශනික හා භක්ති ගීත සඳහා සුදුසු යැයි සැලකේ. ඔහු චිත්‍රපටවල ගීත 100 කට අධික ප්‍රමාණයක් ගායනා කර ඇත. විශ්වනාදන් චිත්‍රපට සහ ටෙලිනාට්‍ය කිහිපයක ද රඟපා ඇත. ඔහුගේ සිනමා මංගල්‍යය වූයේ කමල් හසන් සහ ප්‍රභූදේවා රඟපෑ ‘කාදලා කාදලා’ චිත්‍රපටයයි.

මෙනෙවිය. විශ්වනාදන්ගේ මරණය, ක්ලිචේ භාවිතා කිරීම, යුගයක අවසානය සනිටුහන් කරයි. ඔහු දශක දෙකකට ආසන්න කාලයක් සංගීත නිර්මාපකයෙකු ලෙස අක්‍රියව සිටියද, ශ්‍රව්‍ය සහ දෘශ්‍ය කැසට් සහ යූ ටියුබ් වලට ස්තූතිවන්ත වන්නට මිනිසාගේ සංගීතය සහ ගීත දිගටම පොදු වසමෙහි පැවතුනි.
සැහැල්ලු සංගීතයේ රජවරුන් එම්.එස්. විශ්වනාදන් සහ ටී.කේ. රාමමූර්ති ඔවුන්ගේ ස්වර්ණමය දිනවලදී මට සහ තවත් මිලියන ගණනකට බොහෝ සතුටක් ගෙන දුන්නේය. ඔවුන්ගේ සංගීතය සහ මතකය සදාකාලික වේවා!
“ස්පොට්ලයිට්” තීරුව සඳහා ලියන ලද මෙම ලිපිය 2015 ජූලි 18 වැනි දින “ඩේලි එෆ්ටී” පුවත්පතේ පළ වේ. එයට මෙතැනින් පිවිසිය හැකිය: http://www.ft.lk/d-b-s-jeyaraj

ta.title: “மெல்லிசை மன்னர்கள்” எம்.எஸ். விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் டி.கே. தமிழ் சினிமாவின் ‘ஒளி இசை மன்னர்கள்’ ராமமூர்த்தி
ta.author: நிர்வாகி
body_lines: திருச்சிராப்பள்ளி கிருஷ்ணசாமிப்பிள்ளை ராமமூர்த்தியுடன் (டிகேஆர்) இணைந்து 13 ஆண்டுகளாக தமிழ்த் திரையுலகில் இசையமைத்த பிரபல இசையமைப்பாளர் மனையங்கத் சுப்பிரமணியன் விஸ்வநாதனின் (எம்எஸ்வி) 94வது பிறந்தநாள் ஜூன் 24ஆம் தேதி. எம் எஸ் விஸ்வநாதனும் டிகே ராம்மூர்த்தியும் பிரபலமானவர்கள். "மெல்லிசை மன்னர்கள்" (ஒளி இசையின் மன்னர்கள்) என. ஜூலை 2015 இல் எழுதப்பட்ட இந்தக் கட்டுரை, எம்.எஸ்.வி.யின் 84வது பிறந்தநாளைக் குறிக்கவும், இசை மேஸ்திரிகளின் நினைவுகளைப் புதுப்பிக்கவும் எந்த மாற்றமும் இன்றி மீண்டும் வெளியிடப்படுகிறது.

கொழும்பில் வளர்ந்த சிறுவயதில் தமிழ் திரைப்படங்களை திரையில் பார்ப்பதும் தமிழ் திரைப்பட பாடல்களை வானொலியில் கேட்பதும் வாழ்க்கையின் ஒரு அங்கமாக இருந்தது. தமிழ் ‘வர்தக ஒலிபரப்பு’ அல்லது ‘ரேடியோ சிலோன்’ வணிகச் சேவையானது திரைப்படப் பாடல்களின் பொக்கிஷமாக இருந்தது.

இந்தியாவில் சம்பந்தப்பட்ட திரைப்படங்கள் திரையிடப்படுவதற்கு முன்பே சமீபத்திய தமிழ் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களை ஒளிபரப்புவதைத் தவிர, வணிகச் சேவையானது தீவில் உள்ள திரையரங்குகளில் தற்போது திரையிடப்படும் திரைப்படங்களைப் பற்றிய குறிப்பிட்ட நிகழ்ச்சிகளையும் ஒளிபரப்பியது. பிரபல வானொலி அறிவிப்பாளர் மெயில்வாகனம் இந்த நிகழ்ச்சிகளை நடத்தினார், அங்கு திரைப்படப் பாடல்கள் தொடர்ந்து இடம்பெற்றன. ஆகவே, அந்த அற்புதமான நாட்களில் சமகால தமிழ் திரைப்பட இசை உபயம் ‘ரேடியோ சிலோன்’ பற்றி ஒருவர் அறிந்து கொள்ள முடிந்தது.

வானொலியில் ஒலிக்கும் சில பாடல்களுடன் நானும் என் சகோதரியும் சேர்ந்து பாடுவோம். ‘சினிமா பாடு புத்தகம்’ அல்லது சினிமா பாடல் புத்தகம் இதற்கு பெரிதும் உதவியது. இவை உண்மையில் ஒரு குறிப்பிட்ட தமிழ் திரைப்படத்தின் பாடல்களின் வார்த்தைகளுடன் மலிவான காகிதத்தில் அச்சிடப்பட்ட சிறிய புத்தகங்கள். அன்றைய தமிழ்ப் படங்களில் சராசரியாக ஆறு முதல் ஒன்பது பாடல்கள் இருக்கும்.

கொழும்பில் இரண்டு பதிப்பாளர்கள் எம்.கே. பொன்னையாப்பிள்ளை மற்றும் எம்.எஸ். லிங்கம் (எம். சொக்கலிங்கம்) இந்தப் பாடல் புத்தகங்களை அச்சிட்டார். அப்போது அவற்றின் விலை 10 முதல் 25 காசுகள் வரை இருந்தது. அந்தப் புத்தகங்களிலிருந்து பல திரைப்படப் பாடல்களையும் மனப்பாடம் செய்து கொண்டேன் - இன்றும் ஓய்வு நேரத்தில் குளித்துக்கொண்டிருக்கும் போது அது என்னை நன்றாக நிலைநிறுத்துகிறது.

தமிழ்த் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களைக் கேட்ட இந்த இனிய நினைவுகள் அனைத்தும் தமிழ்த் திரைப்பட இசையின் புகழ்பெற்ற இசையமைப்பாளரின் மரணம் பற்றிய செய்தி என்னை எட்டியபோது மீண்டும் என்னுள் எரிந்தது. இசை மேஸ்திரி எம்.எஸ். "எம்.எஸ்.வி" என்று பரவலாக அறியப்பட்ட விஸ்வநாதன் ஜூலை 14 அன்று அதிகாலை சென்னை மருத்துவமனையில் காலமானார். அவருக்கு வயது 87.

எம்எஸ்வி தமிழில் 600 படங்களுக்கு மேல் இசையமைத்துள்ளார். இந்தத் திரைப்பட இசைத்தொகுப்புகளில் எண்பத்தாறு அவரது முன்னாள் கூட்டாளியான டி.கே. உடன் இணைந்து இசையமைக்கப்பட்டது. ராமமூர்த்தி (டிகேஆர்). மீதமுள்ளவை அனைத்தும் எம்எஸ்வியின் தனி சாதனைகள். விஸ்வநாதனை நினைக்கும் போதெல்லாம் ராமமூர்த்திதான் நினைவுக்கு வருகிறார். விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி, ‘மெல்லிசை’ அல்லது இலகுவான இசை என வர்ணிக்கப்படும் பிரபலமான மெல்லிசைகளை இயற்றுவதன் மூலம் தமிழ் திரைப்பட இசைக்கு பெரிதும் பங்களித்தது. அவர்களின் பல பசுமையான பாடல்கள் இன்னும் நினைவில் நிற்கின்றன.
தேவன் கோவில் மணியோசை - டாக்டர் சீர்காழி சிவசிதம்பரம் மேடையில் எம்எஸ்வி & டிகேஆர் உடனான நேரடி நிகழ்ச்சி

சில ஆண்டுகளுக்கு முன் 2012ல், தமிழக முதல்வர் ஜெயலலிதா ஜெயராம் தலைமையில் நடைபெற்ற விழாவில், விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் ராமமூர்த்திக்கு ‘திரை இசை சக்கரவர்த்தி’ (திரை இசைச் சக்கரவர்த்தி) என்ற பட்டத்தை வழங்கினார். அரசியலுக்கு வருவதற்கு முன்பு தமிழ்த் திரையுலகில் பிரபலமானவர் ஜெயலலிதா. 1948 இல் பிறந்த அவர் எம்எஸ்வி மற்றும் டிகேஆர் ஆகியோரின் இசையுடன் வளர்ந்த தலைமுறையின் ஒரு பகுதியாக இருந்தார்.

இருவரின் இசையால் தான் எவ்வாறு பாதிக்கப்பட்டேன் என்பதை அவர் நிகழ்வில் நினைவு கூர்ந்தார். “நான் இந்தப் படைவீரர்களின் இசையைக் கேட்டு வளர்ந்தவன். அவர்களின் இசை எனது இருப்பின் ஒரு பகுதியாக மாறிவிட்டது, ”என்று முதல்வர் கூறினார். எம்.எஸ்.வி-டி.கே.ஆர் ஜோடியைப் பற்றி ஜெயலலிதா சொன்ன வார்த்தைகள் எனக்குள் பதிலடி கொடுத்தது. முன்பே கூறியது போல் தமிழ் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களை ரசிப்பது என் வளர்ச்சியின் ஒரு பகுதியாகும், மேலும் எம்எஸ்வியின் மரணம் பற்றிய செய்தி ஒரு ஏக்கம் நிறைந்த வெற்றிடத்தை ஏற்படுத்துகிறது. டிகேஆர் 2013 இல் காலமானார்.

மனையங்கத் சுப்பிரமணியன் விஸ்வநாதன் (எம்எஸ்வி) மற்றும் திருச்சிராப்பள்ளி கிருஷ்ணசுவாமிப்பிள்ளை ராமமூர்த்தி (டிகேஆர்) ஆகியோர் 13 வருடங்களாக தமிழ்த் திரைப்படங்களில் இணைந்து ஒரு இசைப் பாதையை சுடர்விட்டனர். விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி இந்தி சினிமாவின் ஷங்கர்-ஜெய்கிஷனுக்கு இணையான தமிழ்ப் படம். உண்மையில் ஷங்கர்-ஜெய்கிஷன் ஜோடிதான் விஸ்வநாதனை ராமமூர்த்தியுடன் இசைக் கூட்டாண்மையை உருவாக்கத் தூண்டியது.

விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி 1952-ம் ஆண்டு உருவானது. இவர்கள் இருவரும் இணைந்து இசையமைத்த முதல் படம் ‘பணம்’, இதில் பிரபல நடிகர்களான சிவாஜி கணேசனும் பத்மினியும் முதன்முறையாக ஹீரோ, ஹீரோயினாக இணைந்து நடித்தனர். எம்.எஸ்.வி-டிகேஆர் ஜோடி 1965 வரை தமிழ் சினிமாவில் இசை அலைகளை உருவாக்கியது. அவர்கள் இணைந்து நடித்த இறுதிப் படம் ‘ஆயிரத்தில் ஒருவன்,’ இதில் எம்.ஜி. ராமச்சந்திரனும், ஜெயலலிதாவும் முதல்முறையாக முக்கிய வேடங்களில் நடித்தனர். பிரிந்து முப்பது ஆண்டுகளுக்குப் பிறகு, சத்யராஜ் நடித்த ‘எங்கிருந்தோ வந்தான்’ படத்திற்காக மீண்டும் எம்எஸ்வி மற்றும் டிகேஆர் இணைந்து நடித்தனர். எம்எஸ்வியும் டிகேஆரும் இணைந்து 13 வருடங்களில் 87 படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்துள்ளனர்.

1922ல் பிறந்த ராமமூர்த்தி இருவரில் மூத்தவர். 1928 இல் பிறந்த விஸ்வநாதன் ஆறு வயது இளையவர். இசையமைப்பாளர் சி.ஆர்.சுப்புராமனின் இசைக்குழுவில் இணைந்து பணியாற்றிய போது இருவரும் நண்பர்களானார்கள். 1952 இல் 28 வயதில் சுப்புராமன் எதிர்பாராத மரணத்தைச் சந்தித்தபோது, ​​விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் தங்கள் குருவின் முடிக்கப்படாத வேலையை முடிக்க வேண்டியிருந்தது. விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் இணைந்து ‘தேவதாஸ்,’ ‘காதல்,’ ‘சொர்க வாசல்’ மற்றும் ‘சண்டி ராணி’ ஆகிய படங்களில் சில பாடல்களுக்கு இசையமைத்துள்ளனர். நல்லது செய்தார்கள்.

ஒரு நாள் இரவு மிட்லாண்ட் திரையரங்கில் படம் பார்த்துவிட்டு திரும்பும் போது, ​​விஸ்வநாதன் அவர்கள் சம பங்குதாரர்களாக இணைந்து படங்களுக்கு இசையமைக்க பரிந்துரைத்தார். ராமமூர்த்தி இசையமைப்பாளராக இருப்பதில் மகிழ்ச்சி அடைவதாகக் கூறினார். இருப்பினும் விஸ்வநாதன் ராமமூர்த்தியை அந்த முன்மொழிவை ஏற்கும்படி வற்புறுத்தினார்.

ஒரு ஜோடியாக வெளிவருவதற்கான இந்த யோசனையை மூத்த நகைச்சுவை நடிகர் என்.எஸ். கிருஷ்ணன். அவர் தயாரித்து வரும் ‘பணம்’ படத்துக்கு இருவரையும் இசையமைப்பாளர்களாக அமர்த்தினார். டி.கே.ஆர் பல வருடங்களில் மூத்தவராக இருந்ததால் பார்ட்னர்ஷிப்பை ராமமூர்த்தி-விஸ்வநாதன் ஜோடி என்று அழைக்க விஸ்வநாதன் விரும்பினார். என். எஸ். கிருஷ்ணன் அவர்கள் தங்களை விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி என்று அழைக்க வேண்டும் என்று வலியுறுத்தினார், இளைய எம்எஸ்விக்கு முன்னுரிமை அளித்தார். ராமமூர்த்திக்கு எந்த எதிர்ப்பும் இல்லை. விஸ்வநாதன், ராமமூர்த்தி இருவரும் கோயம்புத்தூர் மாரியம்மன் கோவிலில் பிரியமாட்டோம் என்று சபதம் எடுத்தனர். எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் ஜோடி பிறந்தது!

திரைப்படப் பாடல்களை பிரபலப்படுத்தி தமிழ் சினிமாவுக்கு மகத்தான பங்களிப்பை வழங்கிய விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி. 'மெல்லிசை' அல்லது 'லைட் மியூசிக்' என்று அழைக்கப்படும் படங்களில் ஒரு நாவல் இசை வகையை உருவாக்கி பரப்புவதில் இருவரும் குறிப்பிடத்தக்க பங்கைக் கொண்டிருந்தனர். இதுவரை தமிழ் திரைப்படங்களில் தாக்கத்தை ஏற்படுத்திய பாரம்பரிய கர்நாடக மற்றும்/அல்லது ஹிந்துஸ்தானி பாரம்பரிய இசை வடிவங்களில் இருந்து இது ஒரு புத்துணர்ச்சியூட்டும் புறப்பாடு ஆகும்.

சராசரி திரைப்பட ரசிகனுக்கு திரைப்படப் பாடல்களின் ஈர்ப்பை அதிகரிக்க இசையமைப்பாளர்கள் பாரம்பரிய இசையின் சில மரபுசார்ந்த இறுக்கங்களை வழங்கத் தொடங்கினர். இத்தகைய இசையமைப்புகள் ‘ஒளி செவ்வியல் இசை’ என்று விவரிக்கப்பட்டன. தமிழ்த் திரைப்படங்களில் இவ்வகையான இலகுவான செவ்வியல் இசைக்கு முன்னோடியாக இருந்தவர்களில் சி.ஆர்.சுப்புராமன், எஸ்.வி. வெங்கட்ராமன், ஜி.ராமநாதன், ஆர்.சுதர்சனம் மற்றும் எஸ்.எம். சுப்பையா நாயுடு.

விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் இந்த இலகுவான கிளாசிக்கல் இசைப் பாதையைத் தொடர்ந்தனர், ஆனால் நிலையான கிளாசிக்கலுக்கு மாறாக இசையின் இலகுவான மெல்லிசை மற்றும் தாள அம்சங்களை வலியுறுத்துவதன் மூலம் தங்களுடைய குறிப்பிட்ட முத்திரையைச் சேர்த்தனர். அவர்களின் அணுகுமுறை தத்தெடுப்பு மற்றும் தகவமைப்புக்கு மிகவும் நெகிழ்வானதாக இருந்தது. ஒரு கிளாசிக்கல் ராகம் பாடலின் அடிப்படையை உருவாக்கியபோதும், இருவரும் அதை இலகுவான அளவில் மாற்றியமைத்தனர். அவர்கள் மற்ற இசை வடிவங்களை அறிமுகப்படுத்தி, பாடல்களில் தடையின்றி நெய்தனர். பாடல்கள் பெரும்பாலும் ஒரு இணக்கமான கலவையுடன் கலகலப்பான மெல்லிசைகளாக இருந்தன.

எட்டுத் திசைகளிலும் மக்கள் சென்று பிறநாட்டு கலைச் செல்வங்களை இங்கு கொண்டு வர வேண்டும் என்று தமிழ்ப் பெருந்தகை சுப்பிரமணிய பாரதி தனது ஒரு பாடலில் வாழ்த்தினார் (செந்தரிடுவீர் எட்டுத்திக்கும் பிறநாட்டுக் கலைச்செல்வங்கள் கொணர்ந்திங்கு சேர்ப்பீர்).

பாரதி பிற நாடுகளின் கலைப் பொக்கிஷங்களைச் சூறையாடுவதை ஆதரிக்கவில்லை, மாறாக ஒரு நாடுகடந்த கலை பரிமாற்றத்திற்கு அழைப்பு விடுத்தார். இதைத்தான் எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் ஜோடி சாதித்தது. உலகின் எல்லாப் பகுதிகளிலும் உள்ள இசை வடிவங்களைத் தேடி, தமிழ்த் திரைப்பட இசைக்கு அறிமுகப்படுத்தினார்கள்.

ராமமூர்த்தியை விட விஸ்வநாதன் இத்தகைய ‘சர்வதேச’ முயற்சிகளில் சிறந்து விளங்கினார். இந்திய அல்லாத இசையை தமிழ் திரைப்படங்களில் முதன்முதலில் அறிமுகப்படுத்திய இசையமைப்பாளர், சில தமிழ் படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்த பிரபல வீணை விரிவுரையாளர் எஸ்.பாலச்சந்தர் ஆவார் என்பது குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது. சி.ஆர்.சுப்புராமனும் பின்பற்றினார். விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் ஆரம்பத்தில் சுப்புராமனைப் பின்பற்றினர், ஆனால் விரைவில் தங்கள் தனித்துவமான அணுகுமுறையை உருவாக்கினர்.

நடன இசை உட்பட பலவிதமான இசை வடிவங்களில் இருந்து உத்வேகம் பெறும் பரந்த அளவிலான ஒலிகள் மற்றும் பாணிகளுக்கு இருவரும் சென்றனர். மேற்கத்திய பாரம்பரிய இசை, மேற்கத்திய நாட்டுப்புற இசை, நற்செய்தி பாடல்கள், பாலாட்கள், ப்ளூஸ், நாட்டுப்புற இசை ஆன்மா இசை, ஜாஸ், வால்ட்ஸ், ராக் அண்ட் ரோல், ரம்பா, ஃபிளமெங்கோ, சாங்குய், மாம்போ, டான்சன், குராச்சா, சா சா சா, லம்படா போன்றவை. பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டது. ஆப்ரோ-அமெரிக்கன், ஆப்ரோ-கியூபன், கியூபன், லத்தீன்-அமெரிக்கன் மற்றும் ஐரோப்பிய இசை வடிவங்களின் மாற்றங்கள் இருவரின் இசை உத்வேகத்திற்காக பெரிதும் நம்பியிருந்தன.

அதே நேரத்தில் விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி தமிழ் இசை மரபிலும் உறுதியாக வேரூன்றி இருந்தது. மாற்றியமைக்கப்பட்ட கர்னாடிக் மற்றும் ஹிந்துஸ்தானி ராகங்களைப் பயன்படுத்துவதைத் தவிர, அவர்கள் தமிழ் கிராமிய இசையையும் பயன்படுத்தினர். ‘கும்மி’ நாட்டுப்புற அடிகள், ‘காவடிச்சிந்து’ பாடல்கள், ‘கிளிக்கண்ணி’ வகை கிராமியப் பாடல்கள், ‘துக்கடா’ கிளாசிக்கல் துணுக்குகள் போன்றவையும் தங்களின் இசையில் எங்கு, எப்பொழுது பொருத்தமானதோ அங்கெல்லாம் இணைக்கப்பட்டன. கிழக்கு மற்றும் மேற்கத்திய இசை வடிவங்கள் இணக்கமாக கலந்தன.

ஒரு வகையில் விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி தமிழ் சினிமாவில் ஃப்யூஷன் இசைக்கு முன்னோடியாக இருந்தது. ‘பதிபக்தி’ படத்தில் வரும் நகைச்சுவைப் பாடல் காட்சியே இதற்குச் சிறந்த உதாரணம். இந்தப் பாடல் ராக் அண்ட் ரோல் மற்றும் கர்நாடக இசையின் கலவையாகும். நகைச்சுவை நடிகர் ஜே.பி.சந்திரபாபு தனது சொந்தக் குரலில் ராக் ‘என்’ ரோல் ஓபனிங் மற்றும் அது தொடர்பான வசனங்களைப் பாடுகிறார். முறையே சிவபெருமானையும் விஷ்ணுவையும் போற்றும் இரண்டு கர்னாடக வசனங்கள் குறுக்கிடப்பட்டுள்ளன. கர்நாடகக் கூறு பாடியவர் வி.என். டி.எஸ்ஸுக்கு குரல் கொடுத்த சுந்தரம். காட்சியில் பாலையா.

இசையமைப்பிற்கான உத்வேகத்தைத் தேடுவதில் வெளிப்படுத்தப்பட்ட தேர்ந்தெடுக்கப்பட்ட சுவைக்கு கூடுதலாக, விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி இரட்டையர்கள் தங்கள் வேலையில் பரந்த அளவிலான இசைக்கருவிகளைப் பயன்படுத்தத் தொடங்கினர். ஹார்மோனியம், வயலின், புல்லாங்குழல், டிரம், தபேலா, பியானோ, வீணை போன்ற வழக்கமான கருவிகளைத் தவிர, இருவரும் கிளாரினெட், செலோ, ஓபோ, சாக்ஸபோன், ட்ரம்பெட் கிட்டார், உகுலேலே மாண்டலின், பாஞ்சோ, பியானோ போன்ற பிற கருவிகளுக்கும் சென்றனர். துருத்தி மற்றும் போங்கோ டிரம்ஸ் அவர்களின் மதிப்பெண்களில் அவற்றை அதிகம் பயன்படுத்துகின்றன.

மற்ற இசையமைப்பாளர்களும் திரைப்படங்களில் இத்தகைய கருவிகளைப் பயன்படுத்தியுள்ளனர், ஆனால் விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி இரட்டையர்கள் முன் எப்போதும் இல்லாத வகையில் அவற்றை பிரமாண்டமாகப் பயன்படுத்தினர். தாள வாத்தியத்தில் பன்முகத்தன்மை விஸ்வநாதனின் தனிச்சிறப்பாக இருந்தது. தபேலாவும் போங்கோவும் அவருக்குப் பிடித்தமானவை.

ஆப்பிரிக்க, லத்தீன் அமெரிக்க மற்றும் ஐரோப்பிய இசையைத் தவிர, விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடி அரபு, பாரசீக மற்றும் ஆப்கானிய இசை வடிவங்களையும் பயன்படுத்தினர். இருவரும் வட இந்திய நாட்டுப்புற இசை வடிவங்களையும் பயன்படுத்தினர். பஞ்சாபி பாங்க்ரா இசை மற்றும் குவாவாலி பக்தி சூஃபி இசையும் சில நேரங்களில் தழுவப்பட்டது. இருவரும் ஷெனாய், சித்தார், சாரங்கி மற்றும் சரோட் போன்ற வட இந்திய இசைக்கருவிகளையும் தங்கள் சில பாடல்களுக்குப் பயன்படுத்தினர்.

குறிப்பாக ஷெனாய் பாத்தோஸை ஒரு கூர்மையான முறையில் வெளிப்படுத்த பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டது. ‘கர்ணன்’ படத்துக்காக சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன் பாடிய ‘உள்ளத்தில் நல்ல உள்ளம்’ பாடல் ராகச் சக்கரவாஹத்தில் (ஹிந்துஸ்தானியில் அஹிர் பைரவ்) எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் ஜோடியால் இயற்றப்பட்டது. பிஸ்மில்லா கான் நடித்த ஷெனாய், மரணக் காட்சியில் சோகமான மகாபாரத உருவம் கர்ணன் மீது அனுதாபமான இரக்க உணர்வுகளைத் தூண்டுவதற்காக இருவரும் தாராளமாகப் பயன்படுத்துகிறார்கள்.

‘பாலும் பழமும்’ படத்தில் பி.சுசீலா பாடிய ‘ஆலயமணியின் ஓசையை நான் கேட்டேன்’ பாடலிலும் இதே கருவியை எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் ஜோடி பயன்படுத்தியிருக்கிறது. இந்த வரிசையில் நடிகை சரோஜா தேவி திரையில் பாடும்போது, ​​ஒரு புகழ்பெற்ற காலையின் அமைதியான மனநிலையை வெளிப்படுத்த ஷெனாயின் ஒலிகள் நன்றாக உதவுகின்றன.

ராமமூர்த்தி ஒரு புகழ்பெற்ற வயலின் கலைஞர். விஸ்வநாதன் ஆர்மோனியம் என்று அழைக்கப்படும் 'பெட்டி' வாசித்தார். பின்னர் அவர் பியானோ மற்றும் விசைப்பலகையில் தேர்ச்சி பெற்றார். அவர்கள் தங்கள் கருவிகளில் வெவ்வேறு பதிப்புகளை வாசிப்பதன் மூலம் ஒரு குறிப்பிட்ட டியூனில் வேலை செய்வார்கள்.

ராமமூர்த்தி பின்னர் குறிப்புகளை எழுதுவார். பின்னர் அவர்கள் தங்கள் இசைக்கலைஞர்கள் மற்றும் பின்னணிப் பாடகர்களுக்கு மெல்லிசைப் பாடுவார்கள் அல்லது இசைப்பார்கள். விஸ்வநாதனுக்குக் காதைக் கவரும் வகையிலும், கால்விரல்களைத் தட்டுகிற வகையிலும் இசையமைப்பதில் திறமை இருந்தது. ராமமூர்த்தி எந்தெந்த கருவிகளைப் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும், எப்படிப் பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும் என்று இசை அமைப்பாளராக இருந்தார். அவர்கள் ஒருவரையொருவர் பூர்த்தி செய்தார்கள்.

விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி அவர்களின் திரைப்பட இசையமைப்பில் பல்வேறு இசைக்கருவிகளைப் பயன்படுத்தினர். பாடல் மற்றும் வரிசையின் தன்மையால் பயன்பாடு தீர்மானிக்கப்பட்டது. உதாரணமாக ‘காத்திருந்த கண்கள்’ படத்தில் சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன் பாடிய ‘ஓடம் நதியினிலே’ பாடலில் ஒரு தபேலாவும் இரண்டு புல்லாங்குழலும் மட்டுமே உள்ளது. இன்னொரு பாடலான ‘தாழையாம் பூமுடிச்சு’ பி.லீலாவும் டி.எம். ‘பாகபிரிவினை’ படத்தில் சௌந்தரராஜன் மூன்று கருவிகளை மட்டுமே நம்பியிருந்தார்.

அதே இருவரும் டி.எம் பாடிய ‘எங்கே நிம்மதி’ பாடலுக்கு 300 பேர் கொண்ட ஆர்கெஸ்ட்ராவைப் பயன்படுத்தினர். ‘புதிய பறவை’யில் சௌந்தரராஜன். ‘களங்கரை விளக்கம்’ படத்தில் சீர்காலி கோவிந்தராஜன் மற்றும் பி.சுசீலா பாடிய ‘சங்கை முழங்கு’ என்ற பாரதிதாசன் பாடலுக்கு 100க்கும் மேற்பட்ட வாத்தியங்கள் பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டன. ராமமூர்த்தி பெரும்பாலான சந்தர்ப்பங்களில் ஆர்கெஸ்ட்ராவை நடத்தினார்.

விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி ஜோடியின் இந்த இசை உல்லாசப் பயணங்கள் அன்றைய தமிழ்த் திரைப்பட இசைக்குக் குறிப்பிடப்படாத பிரதேசமாக இருந்த பகுதிக்கு திரைப்படப் பாடல்களில் ஒரு தரமான மாற்றத்தைக் கொண்டு வந்தன. மெல்லிசைகள் ஆத்மார்த்தமாகவும் மயக்கும் வகையிலும் இருந்தாலும் வசீகரிக்கும் பொதுவான தொடர்பைக் கொண்டிருந்தன.

அந்த நாட்களில் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களும் படத்தின் ஒருங்கிணைந்த பகுதியாக இருந்தன, மேலும் அவை பெரும்பாலும் கதையை திறம்பட விவரிக்க இயக்குனர்களால் பயன்படுத்தப்பட்டன. தற்காலத்தைப் போலல்லாமல், அப்போது படங்களில் பாடல்கள் முழுமையாகப் படமாக்கப்பட்டன. பாடல் காட்சிகள் படத்தின் ஒரு பகுதியாக இருந்தன. பல சந்தர்ப்பங்களில் ஒரு படத்தின் வணிக வெற்றிக்கு பாடல்கள் பெரிதும் உதவியது.

ஒரு திரைப்படப் பாடலை உருவாக்குவது ஒரு குழு முயற்சி. இயக்குனர், பாடலாசிரியர் மற்றும் இசையமைப்பாளர் ஆகியோர் தங்களுக்குள் ஒரு பாடல் தேவைப்படுவதைப் பற்றி விவாதிப்பார்கள். அரிதான நிகழ்வுகளைத் தவிர, பாடல்கள் படமாக்கப்பட்ட காட்சிகள் படமாக்கப்படுவதற்கு முன்பே பதிவு செய்யப்பட்டன.

வார்த்தைகள் முதலில் எழுதப்பட வேண்டுமா, அடுத்ததாக இசையமைக்க வேண்டுமா அல்லது ட்யூன்களை முதலில் இயற்ற வேண்டுமா, அதன்பின் வார்த்தைகள் எழுதப்பட வேண்டுமா என்பதை விவாதங்கள் தீர்மானிக்கும். விஸ்வநாதன் இந்த செயல்முறையை இலகுவான நரம்புகளில் "மீட்டருக்கான விஷயம் அல்லது பொருளுக்கு மீட்டர்" என்று குறிப்பிடுவார்.

MSV-TKR ஜோடி, திரைப்படப் பாடல்களை ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளும் வகையிலும், சாதாரண திரையுலகினருக்குப் பிடிக்கும் வகையிலும், அந்த எண்களை தாங்களாகவே பாட ஆரம்பித்து, அதைத் தங்கள் வாழ்வில் பயன்படுத்துவதில் பெரிய அளவில் வெற்றி பெற்றனர். இந்தப் பாடல்கள் அன்றாட வாழ்க்கையின் ஒரு அங்கமாகிவிட்டன.

பிறப்பு, இறப்பு, காதல், திருமணம், மனவேதனை, பிரிவு, மறு இணைவு, நட்பு, சோகம், மகிழ்ச்சி கொண்டாட்டம், உடன்பிறந்த பாசம், சகோதர-சகோதரி பாசம், தாய் அன்பு, கணவன்-மனைவி உறவு போன்ற வாழ்க்கையின் அனைத்து அம்சங்களையும் அல்லது கட்டங்களையும் குறிக்கும் பாடல்கள் இருந்தன; வாழ்க்கையின் ஒவ்வொரு சந்தர்ப்பத்திற்கும் ஒவ்வொரு மனநிலைக்கும் ஒரு தமிழ் திரைப்படப் பாடல் இருந்தது. எலக்ட்ரானிக்ஸ் வாழ்க்கையின் ஒவ்வொரு அம்சத்தையும் ஆக்கிரமிக்காத ஒரு காலகட்டத்தில், பல கூட்டங்களில் பாடல்கள் ஏராளமாக இருந்தன, தமிழ் திரைப்படப் பாடல்கள் அத்தகைய சந்தர்ப்பங்களில் ஆட்சி செய்தன.

MSV-TKR பாடலின் இசை முன்னுரைகள் மற்றும் இடையிசைகள் கூட சுவாரஸ்யமாக இருந்தன மற்றும் தனக்கென ஒரு அடையாளத்தைக் கொண்டிருந்தன. விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் ராமமூர்த்தி இசையமைத்த ஒரு பாடலை நீங்கள் சில வரிகள் அல்லது இசைக் குறிப்புகளைக் கேட்டவுடன் அடையாளம் காண முடியும். 1960 முதல் 66 வரையிலான காலகட்டத்தை தமிழ்த் திரைப்படப் பாடல்களின் பொற்காலம் எனச் சொல்லலாம்.

விஸ்வநாதன்-ராமமூர்த்தி கூட்டணியில் கே.வி. அந்தப் பொற்காலத்தில் திரைப்பட இசைத் துறையில் மகாதேவன் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தினார். 1960 மற்றும் 66 க்கு இடையில் விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் ஆட்சி செய்தனர். அவர்களின் புத்துணர்ச்சியூட்டும் லைட் மியூசிக் அல்லது மெல்லிசை பலரின் வாழ்க்கையை உண்மையிலேயே ஒளிரச் செய்தது.

இந்த ஒளி இசையின் புகழ் 16 ஜூன் 1963 அன்று டிரிப்ளிகேனில் உள்ள கலாச்சார அகாடமியில் நடைபெற்ற ஒரு சிறப்பு நிகழ்வில் முறையாக ஒப்புக்கொள்ளப்பட்டது. ‘மெல்லிசை மன்னர்’ (ஒளி இசை மன்னர்) என்ற பட்டம் எம்.எஸ். விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் டி.கே. நடிகர்களின் தலைவரான சிவாஜி கணேசனால் ராமமூர்த்தி. இரண்டு இசையமைப்பாளர்களும் ஒளி இசையின் மன்னர்களாக முடிசூட்டப்பட்டதைக் கண்டு புகழ்பெற்ற திரையுலகப் பிரமுகர்கள் மகிழ்ச்சியுடன் கைதட்டினர்.

ஐயோ! இரண்டே ஆண்டுகளில் உறவில் விரிசல் ஏற்பட்டது. செல்வி. விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் டி.கே. 1965 ஆம் ஆண்டு ஜூலை மாதம் ‘ஆயிரத்தில் ஒருவன்’ திரைப்படம் வெளியான பிறகு ராமமூர்த்தி அவர்களின் இசை கூட்டணியை கலைத்தார். பிரிந்ததற்கான காரணங்களாக பல கோட்பாடுகள் முன்வைக்கப்பட்டன. திரைப்பட இயக்குநர்கள், பாடலாசிரியர்கள், பின்னணிப் பாடகர்கள் மற்றும் நடிகர்கள் என ஒரு கும்பல் ஈகோ மோதல் ஏற்பட்டபோது இருவருக்கும் இடையே பிளவை ஏற்படுத்தியது. விஸ்வநாதனும் ராமமூர்த்தியும் இவ்விஷயத்தில் கண்ணியமான மௌனம் காத்தனர். இருப்பினும் இந்த பிரிவு தமிழ் திரையுலகிற்கு பெரும் இழப்பு என்பதில் சந்தேகமில்லை. எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் பிரிவின் போது நான் அடைந்த பெரும் சோகத்தை என்னால் நினைவுகூர முடிகிறது.

எம்.எஸ்.வி மற்றும் டி.கே.ஆர் இருவரும் தனித்தனியாக அந்தந்த இசைப் பயணத்தைத் தொடர்ந்தனர். இருவரும் தங்கள் ஒளி இசையால் திரைப்பட ரசிகர்களை மகிழ்வித்தாலும், அவர்களின் அதிர்ஷ்டம் வேறுபட்டது. பிரிந்த பிறகு ராமமூர்த்தி தனது திரையுலக வாழ்க்கை குறைவதைக் கண்டார். அவர் இசையமைக்க குறைவான வாய்ப்புகளைப் பெறத் தொடங்கினார். TKR சிறப்பாக இசையமைத்தார் ஆனால் துரதிர்ஷ்டவசமாக சம்பந்தப்பட்ட பெரும்பாலான படங்கள் வணிக ரீதியாக தோல்வியடைந்தன. இதனால் டி.கே.ஆரின் தனி திரைப்பட வாழ்க்கை சில வருடங்களில் கிட்டத்தட்ட முடிவுக்கு வந்தது. எம்எஸ்வியை பிரிந்த பிறகு 16 தமிழ் படங்களுக்கு மட்டுமே இசையமைத்தார். 1995-ம் ஆண்டு எம்எஸ்வி-டிகேஆர் கூட்டணி மீண்டும் ‘எங்கிருந்தோ வந்தான்’ படத்தில் இணைந்தது. இசை ஏமாற்றமாக இருந்தது. பழைய மந்திரம் போய்விட்டது.

ராமமூர்த்தியின் தலைவிதிக்கு மாறாக, அவர் தனித்துப் போன பிறகு விஸ்வநாதனின் திரையுலக வாழ்க்கை மெழுகியது. பிரிந்த பிறகு எம்.எஸ்.விக்கு படங்களுக்கு இசையமைக்கும் வாய்ப்புகள் அதிகம். குறிப்பிடத்தக்க தனிப்பட்ட திறன்களைக் கொண்ட விஸ்வநாதன் திரைப்படப் பணிகளை எளிதாகப் பெற முடிந்தது.

செல்வி. விஸ்வநாதன் 1965 முதல் 1975 வரை தமிழ் திரைப்பட இசை உலகில் ஆதிக்கம் செலுத்தினார். அந்த பத்தாண்டுகளில் ஒவ்வொரு ஆண்டும் சுமார் 20 படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்தார். 1976-ல் ‘அன்னக்கிளி’ வழியாக இளையராஜாவின் வருகைக்குப் பிறகு, விஸ்வநாதனின் மெய்நிகர் ஏகபோகம் சவாலுக்கு உள்ளானது. 1985 வரை இளையராஜாவின் தாக்குதலுக்கு எதிராக விஸ்வநாதன் ஒவ்வொரு ஆண்டும் குறைந்தது 10 படங்களுக்கு இசையமைத்தார்.

1985க்குப் பிறகு விஸ்வநாதனுக்குப் படங்கள் எண்ணிக்கை குறையத் தொடங்கியது. ஏ.ஆருக்குப் பிறகு இது ஒரு துளிர்விட்டது. ரஹ்மானும் 1992ல் ‘ரோஜா’ மூலம் களம் இறங்கினார். கடைசியாக எம்.எஸ். விஸ்வநாதன் இசையமைத்த ‘வாலிபன் சுற்றும் உலகம்’ 2012ல் வெளியானது.

சிறுவயதில் விஸ்வநாதன் பாடகராக வேண்டும் என்று விரும்பினார். இளமைப் பருவத்தில் அவர் நடிகராக ஆசைப்பட்டார். இரண்டு கனவுகளும் நனவாகவில்லை, விஸ்வநாதன் ஒரு புகழ்பெற்ற இசையமைப்பாளராக ஆனார். எவ்வாறாயினும், பிற்கால வாழ்க்கையில் எம்எஸ்வி பாடகராகவும் நடிகராகவும் மாற வேண்டும் என்று விதி விதித்தது.

விஸ்வநாதன் முதலில் தனது சொந்தப் படங்களில் சில பாடல்களைப் பாடத் தொடங்கினார். பின்னர் மற்ற இசையமைப்பாளர்களும் அவரைப் பயன்படுத்தத் தொடங்கினர். அவரது ஆழமான எதிரொலிக்கும் குரல் தத்துவ மற்றும் பக்தி பாடல்களுக்கு பொருத்தமானதாக கருதப்பட்டது. திரைப்படங்களில் 100 பாடல்களுக்கு மேல் பாடியுள்ளார். விஸ்வநாதன் சில படங்கள் மற்றும் டெலி நாடகங்களிலும் நடித்துள்ளார். கமல்ஹாசன் மற்றும் பிரபுதேவா நடித்த ‘காதலா காதலா’ படத்தின் மூலம் இவரது அறிமுகம் ஆனது.

செல்வி. விஸ்வநாதனின் மரணம், ஒரு சகாப்தத்தின் முடிவைக் குறிக்கிறது. ஏறக்குறைய இரண்டு தசாப்தங்களாக அவர் ஒரு இசையமைப்பாளராக செயலற்ற நிலையில் இருந்தபோதிலும், ஆடியோ மற்றும் வீடியோ கேசட்டுகள் மற்றும் யூடியூப் மூலம் அந்த மனிதனின் இசை மற்றும் பாடல்கள் பொது களத்தில் தொடர்ந்து இருந்தன.

ஒளி இசையின் மன்னர்களான எம்.எஸ். விஸ்வநாதன் மற்றும் டி.கே. ராமமூர்த்தி அவர்களின் பொன்னான நாட்களில் எனக்கும் மில்லியன் கணக்கானவர்களுக்கும் மிகுந்த மகிழ்ச்சியைத் தந்தார். அவர்களின் இசையும் நினைவும் என்றென்றும் நிலைத்திருக்கட்டும்!
“ஸ்பாட்லைட்” நெடுவரிசைக்காக எழுதப்பட்ட இந்தக் கட்டுரை ஜூலை 18, 2015 இன் “டெய்லி எஃப்டி”யில் வெளிவந்துள்ளது. இதை இங்கே அணுகலாம்: http://www.ft.lk/d-b-s-jeyaraj

title: “Mellisai Mannargal” M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy, the ‘Monarchs of Light Music’ in Tamil cinema
body_lines: (June 24th is the 94th birth anniversary of popular music composer Manayangath Subramanian Viswanathan (MSV) who along with  Tiruchirappalli Krishnaswamypillai Ramamurthy (TKR) blazed a musical trail together in Tamil films for a period of 13 years. MS Viswanathan and TK Rammmurthy were known popularly as the “Mellisai Mannargal” (Monarchs of light music).This Article written in July 2015 is being re-posted without any changes to  denote MSV’s 84th Birth Anniversary and revive memories of the music  maestros)

Watching Tamil movies on screen and listening to Tamil film songs over radio was an integral part of life for me as a child growing up in Colombo. The Tamil ‘Varthaga Oliparappu’ or Commercial service of ‘Radio Ceylon’ was a treasure trove of film songs.

Apart from broadcasting the latest Tamil film songs even before the films concerned were screened in India, the commercial service also aired specific programs about films being currently shown in theatres in the island. Popular radio announcer Mailvaganam conducted these programs where film songs were featured regularly. Thus one was able to keep abreast of contemporary Tamil film music courtesy ‘Radio Ceylon’ in those wonderful days of yore.

My sister and I would also sing along with some songs being played out on radio. We were greatly aided in this by what was known as ‘Cinema Paatu Puthagam’ or cinema songbook. These were actually tiny booklets printed on cheap paper with the words of songs in a particular Tamil film. The average Tamil film in those days had about six to nine songs.

Two publishers in Colombo M.K. Ponniahpillai and M.S. Lingam (M. Sockalingam)  printed these songbooks. They were priced from 10 to 25 cents then. I also memorised many film songs from those books – something which stands me in good stead even nowadays while taking a shower at leisure.

All these happy memories of listening to Tamil film songs were kindled in me again as news about the death of a legendary composer of Tamil film music reached me. Music maestro M.S. Viswanathan known widely as “MSV” had passed away at a Chennai hospital in the early hours of the morning on 14 July. He was 87.

MSV has scored music for over 600 films in Tamil. Eighty-six of these film scores were composed jointly with his erstwhile partner T.K. Ramamurthy (TKR). The rest were all solo feats by MSV. Whenever I think of Viswanathan, I am reminded of Ramamurthy. The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo contributed greatly to the Tamil film music by composing popular melodies described as ‘Mellisai’ or light music. Many of their evergreen songs linger in memory still.
Devan Kovil Maniyosai – Live performance with MSV & TKR on stage by Dr. Sirkali SivaChidambaram

A few years back in 2012, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Jayalalithaa Jayaram presided at a ceremony where she conferred the title ‘Thirai Isai Chakkaravarthy’ (Emperor of Screen Music) upon Viswanathan and Ramamurthy. Jayalalithaa was a popular screen heroine in Tamil films before entering politics. Born in 1948 she was part of a generation that grew up with the music of MSV and TKR.

She recalled at the event about how she was influenced by the music of the two. “I grew up listening to the music of these veterans. Their music has become part of my very existence,” the Chief Minister said. Jayalalithaa’s words about the MSV-TKR duo struck a responsive chord in me. As stated earlier relishing Tamil film songs was part of growing up for me and news of MSV’s death does cause a nostalgic void. TKR passed away in 2013.

Manayangath Subramanian Viswanathan (MSV) and Tiruchirappalli Krishnaswamypillai Ramamurthy (TKR) blazed a musical trail together in Tamil films for a period of 13 years. The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo was the Tamil film equivalent to Hindi cinema’s Shankar-Jaikishan. In fact the Shankar-Jaikishan duo was the model which motivated Viswanathan to forge a musical partnership with Ramamurthy.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo came into being in the year 1952. The first film for which the duo composed music was ‘Panam,’ in which noted actors Sivaji Ganesan and Padmini acted together for the first time as hero and heroine. The MSV-TKR duo made musical waves in Tamil cinema until 1965. Their final film together was ‘Aayirathil Oruvan,’ in which M.G. Ramachandran and Jayalalithaa played the lead roles for the first time. Thirty years after the break up, MSV and TKR collaborated again for the film ‘Engirundho Vanthaan’ starring Sathyaraj. MSV and TKR have together composed music for 87 films over a period of 13 years.

Ramamurthy, born in 1922, was the elder of the two. Viswanathan, born in 1928, was six years younger. The two of them became friends while working together in the musical troupe of composer C.R. Subburaman. When Subburaman met with unexpected death at the age of 28 in 1952, Viswanathan and Ramamurthy had to complete their guru’s unfinished work. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy collaborated in composing tunes for a few songs in the films ‘Devadas,’ ‘Kaadhal,’ ‘Sorga Vaasal’ and ‘Chandi Rani’. They did good.

One night while returning after seeing a film at what was then the Midland theatre, Viswanathan suggested that they compose music for films together as equal partners. Ramamurthy demurred saying he was happy to remain a musician. Viswanathan however persuaded Ramamurthy to accept the proposal.

This idea of venturing out as a duo was welcomed by veteran comedian N.S. Krishnan. He hired both as the composers for a film he was producing named ‘Panam’. Viswanathan wanted the partnership to be called the Ramamurthy-Viswanathan duo as TKR was senior in years. N.S. Krishnan however insisted that they should call themselves Viswanathan-Ramamurthy, giving precedence to the younger MSV. Ramamurthy had no objections. Both Viswanathan and Ramamurthy took a vow at the Maariyamman temple in Coimbatore that they would never separate. The MSV-TKR duo was born!

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo made a tremendous contribution to Tamil cinema by popularising film songs. The duo played a significant role in developing and propagating a novel genre of music in films known as ‘Mellisai’ or ‘light music’. This was a refreshing departure from the traditional Carnatic and/or Hindustani classical music forms which hitherto influenced Tamil films.

Music composers began dispensing with some of the orthodox rigidities of classical music in order to increase the appeal of film songs to the average film fan. Such musical compositions were described as ‘light classical music’. Among those who pioneered this type of light classical music in Tamil films were C.R. Subburaman, S.V. Venkatraman, G. Ramanathan, R. Sudharsanam and S.M. Subbiah Naidu.

Viswanathan and Ramamurthy proceeded this light classical music path but added their own particular imprint by emphasising the lighter melodic and rhythmic aspects of music as opposed to the staid classical. Their approach was more flexible being adoptive and adaptive. Even when a classical raga formed the song’s base, the duo modified it on a lighter scale. They introduced other forms of music and wove them seamlessly into the songs. The songs were very often lively melodies with a harmonious blend.

The great Tamil poet Subramania Bharati in one of his verses wished that people would go in all eight directions and fetch the artistic wealth of other countries here (Sendriduveer Ettuthikkum Piranaatuk Kalaichelvangal Konarnthingu Saerppeer).

Bharati was not advocating the plunder of artistic treasures of other countries but merely calling for a transnational artistic exchange. This was exactly what the MSV-TKR duo accomplished. They sought out music forms in all parts of the world and introduced them to Tamil film music.

Viswanathan more than Ramamurthy excelled in such ‘international’ pursuits. It must be noted however that the composer who first introduced non-Indian music into Tamil films was the famous Veena exponent S. Balachandar who scored music for some Tamil films. C.R. Subburaman also followed suit. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy initially emulated Subburaman but soon evolved their own distinctive approach.

The duo went in for a broad range of sounds and styles drawing inspiration from a wide array of musical forms, including dance music. Western classical music, Western country music, gospel songs, ballads, blues, folk music soul music, jazz, waltz, rock and roll, rumba, flamenco, changui, mambo, danzon, guaracha, cha cha cha, lambada, etc. were all utilised. Modifications of Afro-American, Afro-Cuban, Cuban, Latin-American and European musical forms were relied upon greatly for musical inspiration by the duo.

At the same time the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo was firmly rooted to the Tamil musical tradition too. Apart from using modified Carnatic and Hindustani ragas, they also used Tamil rural music. ‘Kummi’ folk beats, ‘Kaavadichchinthu’ ditties, ‘Kilikkanni’ type village songs, ‘Thukkadaa’ classical bits, etc. were also incorporated into their music wherever and whenever appropriate. Eastern and Western music forms were harmoniously blended.

In a sense the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo pioneered fusion music in Tamil cinema. A case in point is the humorous song sequence in the film ‘Pathibakthi’. The song is a combination of rock and roll and Carnatic music. Comedian J.P. Chandrababu sings in his own voice the rock ‘n’ roll opening and related verses. Interspersed are two Carnatic verses in praise of Lord Shiva and Vishnu respectively. The Carnatic component is sung by V.N. Sundaram who voices for T.S. Balaiah in the scene.

In addition to the eclectic taste displayed in seeking inspiration for musical compositions, the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo also began using a wide array of musical instruments in their work. Apart from the regular instruments like harmonium, violin, flute, drum, tabla, piano, veena, etc., the duo also went in for other instruments like the clarinet, cello, oboe, saxophone, trumpet guitar, ukulele mandolin, banjo, piano accordion and bongo drums making much use of them in their scores.

Other composers too have used such instruments in films but the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo utilised them grandly in a manner never done before. Variety in percussion was a particular hallmark of Viswanathan. The tabla and bongos were his favourites.

Apart from African, Latin American and European music, the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo also made use of Arabic, Persian and Afghan music forms. The duo also used North Indian folk music forms. Punjabi Bhangra music and Quawwali Devotional Sufi music were also adapted at times. The duo also used North Indian instruments like the shenoy, sitar, sarangi and sarode for some of their compositions.

The shenoy in particular was used to express pathos in a poignant manner. The song ‘Ullathil Nalla Ullam’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan for the film ‘Karnan’ was composed in the Raga Chakravaham (Ahir Bhairav in Hindustani) by the MSV-TKR duo. The shenoy played by Bismilla Khan is used liberally by the duo to invoke feelings of sympathetic compassion for the tragic Mahabharatha figure Karnan in the death scene.

The same instrument is used by the MSV-TKR duo in the song ‘Aalayamaniyin Osaiyai Naan Kaettaen’ sung by P. Susheela in the film ‘Paalum Palamum’. In this sequence the sounds of the shenoy serve well to bring out the serene mood of a glorious morning as actress Saroja Devi sings on screen.

Ramamurthy was a reputed violinist. Viswanathan played the ‘petti’ as the harmonium was called. Later he mastered piano and keyboard. They would work on a particular tune by playing different versions on their instruments.

Ramamurthy would then write up the notations. They would then sing or play the melodies to their musicians and playback singers. Viswanathan had a flair for composing ear-catching, toe-tapping melodies. Ramamurthy was more of a music arranger working out which instruments were to be used and how. They complemented each other.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy combine used diverse musical instruments in their film compositions. The usage was determined by the nature of the song and sequence. For instance the song ‘Oadam Nathiyiniley’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan in the film ‘Kaathiruntha Kangal’ has only a tabla and two flutes. Another song, ‘Thaalaiyaam Poomudichu’ sung by P. Leela and T.M. Soundararajan in the film ‘Bhagapirivinai,’ also relied on three instruments only.

The same duo however used an orchestra of 300 for the song ‘Engay Nimmathi’ sung by T.M. Soundararajan in ‘Pudhiya Paravai’. Over 100 instruments were used for the Bharatidasan song ‘Sangay Mulangu’ sung by Seergali Govindarajan and P. Susheela in ‘Kalangarai Vilakkam’. Ramamurthy conducted the orchestra on most occasions.

These musical excursions by the Viswanathan-Ramamurthy duo into what was unchartered territory for Tamil film music at that time brought about a qualitative change in film songs. The melodies though soulful and enchanting yet possessed a captivating common touch.

The film songs too in those days were an integral part of the film and often used by directors to narrate the story effectively. Unlike the present, songs were picturised fully in the films then. The song sequences were part and parcel of the film. In many instances the songs contributed greatly to the commercial success of a film.

The making of a film song was a team effort. The director, lyricist and music composer would discuss among themselves about the film sequence for which a song was required. Except for rare instances, the songs were recorded before the scenes on which they are picturised were shot.

Discussions would determine whether the words were to be written first and tunes composed next or the tunes were to be composed first and words written thereafter. Viswanathan would refer to this process in lighter vein as “matter for metre or metre for matter”.

The MSV-TKR duo succeeded to a great extent in making film songs acceptable and endearing to ordinary filmgoers who began singing those numbers themselves and applying them in their lives. These songs became a part of everyday life itself.

There were songs to denote all aspects or phases of life like birth, death, romance, marriage, heartbreak, separation, reunion, friendship, sorrow, joy celebration, sibling affection, brother-sister bonds, motherly love, husband-wife relationship; there was a Tamil film song for every occasion and every mood in life. In an era where electronics had not invaded every facet of life, there were singsongs galore in many gatherings and Tamil film songs ruled the roost on such occasions.

Even the musical preludes and interludes in a MSV-TKR song were impressive and had an identity of their own. One could recognise a song composed by Viswanathan and Ramamurthy the moment you heard a few lines or musical notes. The 1960 to ’66 period could very well be described as the golden age of Tamil film songs.

The Viswanathan-Ramamurthy combination along with K.V. Mahadevan dominated the film music scene in that golden age. Viswanathan and Ramamurthy reigned supreme between 1960 and 66. Their refreshing brand of light music or mellisai truly lightened the lives of many.

The popularity of this light music was acknowledged formally on 16 June 1963 at a special event held at the Cultural Academy in Triplicane. The title of ‘Mellisai Mannar’ (Monarch of Light Music) was conferred on both M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy by the doyen of actors, Sivaji Ganesan. A distinguished gathering of film personalities applauded with delight as the two music composers were crowned as monarchs of light music.

Alas! Within two years the relationship fractured. M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy dissolved their musical partnership in July 1965 after the release of the film ‘Aayirathil Oruvan’. Many theories were floated as causes for the break-up. Apparently a clique of film directors, lyricists, playback singers and actors had driven a wedge between both when a clash of egos occurred. Both Viswanathan and Ramamurthy maintained a dignified silence on the matter. There is no doubt however that the split was a great loss to the Tamil film industry. I can recall the immense sadness I felt at the time of the MSV-TKR split.

Both MSV and TKR continued separately on their respective musical journeys. Though both continued to entertain film fans with their light music, their fortunes differed. Ramamurthy found his film career waning after the break up. He began getting fewer and fewer opportunities to score music. TKR did compose music excellently but unfortunately most of the films concerned turned out to be commercial failures. Thus the solo film career of TKR virtually ended within some years. He composed music for only 16 Tamil films after parting ways with MSV. In 1995 the MSV-TKR duo combined again for the film ‘Engiruntho Vanthaan’. The music was disappointing. The old magic was gone.

Contrary to the fate of Ramamurthy, the film career of Viswanathan waxed after he went solo. MSV got a lot of opportunities to score music for films after the split. Possessing remarkable interpersonal skills, Viswanathan was able to procure film assignments easily.

M.S. Viswanathan dominated the Tamil film music scene from 1965 to 1975. He composed music for about 20 films each year during that decade. After the advent of Illayarajah via ‘Annakkili’ in 1976, Viswanathan’s virtual monopoly was challenged. Still Viswanathan held his own against the Illayarajah onslaught until 1985 composing music for at least 10 films each year.

After 1985 the number of films for Viswanathan began to dwindle. It became a trickle after A.R. Rahman also entered the scene in 1992 through ‘Rojah’. The last film for which M.S. Viswanathan composed music was ‘Vaaliban Suttrum Ulagam’ in 2012.

In his boyhood Viswanathan had wanted to be a singer. In his teens he aspired to be an actor. Both dreams did not materialise and Viswanathan became a reputed music composer. Fate however decreed that MSV become a singer and actor in later life.

Viswanathan started singing a few songs in some of his own films first. Later other music composers also began using him. His deep resonating voice was deemed appropriate for philosophical and devotional songs. He has sung over 100 songs in films. Viswanathan has also acted in a few films and teledramas. His film debut was in ‘Kaadhalaa Kaadhalaa,’ starring Kamal Haasan and Prabhu Deva.

M.S. Viswanathan’s death, to use a cliché, marks the end of an era. Though he was inactive as a music composer for nearly two decades, the man’s music and songs continued to be in the public domain thanks to audio and video cassettes and YouTube.
The monarchs of light music M.S. Viswanathan and T.K. Ramamurthy brought much pleasure to me and millions of others during their golden days. May their music and memory remain forever!
This article written for the “Spotlight” Column appears in the “Daily FT” of July 18, 2015. It can be accessed here: http://www.ft.lk/d-b-s-jeyaraj
